programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Steve accidentally kills a suspect during a routine stake-out, prompting his disillusioned new partner to suggest covering up the death. Medical detective drama, starring Barry Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan and Steve race to find the link between three murder victims before the killer strikes again. Medical detective drama, guest starring George Lazenby

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Susan gets stranded in a small town, where she is aghast to learn her former fiance, believed to be dead, is actually alive and well - presenting Dr Sloan and Jesse with a perplexing mystery to unravel. Dick Van Dyke stars

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Walter learns Cabe's secret about the Baghdad mission and it threatens to tear the team apart. Meanwhile, Paige is shocked when Ralph puts his life in danger to help Scorpion. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: With the team fragmented, everyone feels uncertain about their future, but they are forced to come together again to try to save Walter's life when he has a car accident and his vehicle ends up hanging over the edge of a cliff. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team flies to Israel, but receives a hostile reception from Ziva's father, who is the head of intelligence agency Mossad. As events unfold, Gibbs learns that Ziva was romantically involved with Rivkin and finds himself forced to make a tough decision. Guest starring Michael Nouri, alongside Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony travels to North Africa on the trail of missing agent Ziva, who is thought to have been tracking down a terrorist leader called Saleem Ulman. Intent on exacting revenge for her apparent death during a flight to Somalia, he is taken hostage by militants and discovers she is still alive - only to be given a potentially catastrophic ultimatum. Starring Mark Harmon and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and his team investigate the bizarre deaths of a marine and two of his friends at a stag party. Elsewhere, Gibbs tries to persuade Vance to allow Ziva back into the NCIS. However, she must first pass a barrage of psychological evaluations. Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A blogger who accused the NCIS of a cover-up is found dead, and Gibbs and his team are forced to reopen a closed case to find the killer. However, state police suspect the special agents may have been involved in the murder and take control of the investigation. Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a marine off the coast of Tanzania - a case complicated by the discovery that he had a link to Ziva. The mystery deepens even further with the recovery of wreckage from a sunken freighter. Starring Mark Harmon, Cote de Pablo, David McCallum, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Walter learns Cabe's secret about the Baghdad mission and it threatens to tear the team apart. Meanwhile, Paige is shocked when Ralph puts his life in danger to help Scorpion. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: With the team fragmented, everyone feels uncertain about their future, but they are forced to come together again to try to save Walter's life when he has a car accident and his vehicle ends up hanging over the edge of a cliff. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A taxi driver recently released from prison is murdered just hours after sending an e-mail to an old alias that Callen used when he went undercover to investigate three marines who stole $5million. Resuming the persona, Callen goes in search of the gang, one of whom is killed in a drive-by shooting

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen, Sam and the team spring into action when fellow agent Dom activates a distress signal. They track down his car, which is riddled with bullet holes, and Eric intercepts a signal from his phone, tracing it to a house that contains nothing but some used medical supplies and a dead body. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: After a mid-series break, the drama following the exploits of the Los Angeles-based Office of Special Projects returns. Sam and Callen investigate when five men wearing US Marine uniforms are found dead in a warehouse and discover they were killed by a lethal toxin that could be used as a bioweapon. Initial inquiries lead them to a former military chemist who now has Alzheimer's, while Callen recognises one of the victims as someone who used to work as hired muscle for the well-connected owner of a strip joint. Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates when an Islamic terrorist group in Iraq steals a truck with $5million and track the money back to America. The agents learn the cash has been used to set up a bank robbery intended as a ruse to allow a bank employee to transfer $100million of the terrorists' money that has been frozen by the government in an offshore bank. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Calvin Lee, an executive naval officer, appears to commit suicide by hanging himself from a public road bridge. When examining CCTV footage of the incident, Sam and Callen discover the dead man tossed away a small object before jumping to his death, and when they learn the late lieutenant commander had been working on a sensitive project involving new cloaking technology for submarines, they immediately take an interest in the item. The investigation draws the team into the furtive world of LA's Chinese community, where they discover Lee might have been harbouring some personal as well as professional secrets. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: Scorpion

programme.desc: An unpopular woman is accused of killing her wealthy husband - but luckily for her, she has a twin sister to provide her with a seemingly airtight alibi. Robert Vaughn guest stars

programme.desc: A visiting doctor is suspected of murdering one of his colleagues in an alcoholic stupor. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Lt Dean captures Gibbs in Mexico and hands him over to Paloma Reynosa, head of a drug cartel and daughter of the man he killed. Meanwhile, Ziva is accepted as a US citizen

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker needs to prove that Trivette is not responsible for the death of a six-year-old boy, even though the head of internal affairs believes he is guilty. Starring Chuck Norris

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: US Marine sergeant Teddy Lemere is killed by a sniper in Afghanistan just after his sniffer dog helps guide a young child out of a minefield. Back in the US, his widow Ruby tells Gibbs and the team that she had recently received a message from her husband saying he might have upset a few people. Abby retrieves a tiny shard of the sniper's bullet from Ted's helmet and finds that it was made in America

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A naval officer involved in an illegal underground street race is killed when his vehicle explodes. The team's investigations reveal he was carrying classified equipment in relation to his work on a new form of battery that the military had hoped to adopt, and that the explosion - captured on CCTV - is not in accordance with that expected of normal fuel. Meanwhile, Callen latest driving misdemeanour means he must attend traffic school. Chris O'Donnell stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs's father is increasingly frustrated at being kept in his son's house under armed guard, and Franks joins his security detail after emerging from the convent where he had been hiding out. Meanwhile, Ducky finds evidence at a helicopter crash that gives the team an important lead in tracking down Paloma Reynosa, and the drug kingpin's brother Alejandro may have an ulterior motive when he tries to secure a deal involving her extradition. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of three. The discovery of two more bodies convinces Heaton his suspect is a serial killer, but the man he sent to prison 20 years before sticks to his story that he is innocent. A retired colleague who worked on the original case reveals that vital evidence was withheld at the time of the trial and the pressure mounts to re-examine the prosecution. Bad Girls' James Gaddas guest stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part three of three. Heaton's first suspect provides an alibi for one of the cold case murders and finds himself in the clear as forensic evidence establishes that all three girls were killed in the same manner. Desperate for his freedom, he cooperates with the police by identifying everyone involved in staging the rave events attended by the victims. When the real killer is named, the race is on to find him before he strikes again. James Gaddas guest stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: When a cab driver is badly beaten and his takings stolen, an electronic travel card found near the scene provides a lead, linking the crime to a volunteer worker at a support centre on the Jasmine Allen estate. The suspect is questioned and protests her innocence, but when officers approach her again she runs off, spilling a fair amount of cash in the process

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A teenager is found unconscious in the street following a party that went disastrously wrong when a known drug dealer turned up causing trouble. Nate takes an instant shine to a paramedic called to attend to the victim, but later finds himself obliged to arrest her brother for his involvement in the incident. Rachel McDowall guest stars

programme.title: Jane Eyre
programme.desc: Four-part adaptation of Charlotte Bronte's novel about an orphan girl who struggles to survive the harsh treatment of a boarding school to embark on a career as a governess. Jane takes up her first position at the home of the alluring and unpredictable Edward Rochester, under the watchful eye of laundrywoman Grace Poole. Ruth Wilson, Toby Stephens and Francesca Annis star

programme.title: Jane Eyre
programme.desc: Jane begins to wonder about the chemistry between herself and Rochester, who disappoints her when he leaves without warning. However, he returns with a houseful of distinguished guests, including the beautiful Blanche Ingram and her opinionated mother. Things turn sour when a mysterious visitor arrives. Ruth Wilson, Toby Stephens and Francesca Annis star

programme.title: Jane Eyre
programme.desc: After fulfilling her dying aunt's last wishes, Jane returns to Thornfield Hall - only to find Mr Rochester is set to wed Blanche. However, after she confesses her love, he shares his true feelings and asks for her hand in marriage instead. While all seems well at first, a ghostly figure begins to torment Jane as the wedding day draws near. Drama, with Ruth Wilson and Toby Stephens

programme.title: Jane Eyre
programme.desc: A clergyman and his two sisters take a heartbroken Jane under their wing after finding her alone and starving on the moors. The governess embarks on a new life as a parish teacher, but thinks she hears Rochester call out her name and feels compelled to return to Thornfield - where the secrets of the North Tower are finally revealed. Starring Ruth Wilson and Toby Stephens

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Tide of Life
programme.desc: Gillian Kearney stars in the feature-length period drama following the fortunes of housekeeper Emily Kennedy and her relationships with three very different men. At first she is content working for the McGilby family while her father is away at sea, but finds her life thrown into turmoil by the death of one of her employers. With John Bowler and Ray Stevenson

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Barbara helps a pregnant mother-of-two whose husband has recently died, and is on hand to give her support when a further tragedy drives her and her children from their home. Lucille teaches a health and relationships class at the youth club, only to encounter a furious mother who objects to her daughter attending. Sister Monica Joan goes into hospital for cataract surgery

programme.title: Zen
programme.desc: Detective drama, based on the best-selling novels by Michael Dibdin. Aurelio Zen begins to feel like he is the only honest policeman in Rome, as he sees the city becoming overrun by corruption and deceit - and is plunged into the thick of a political scandal when he is sent to re-investigate a murder in a remote village. As pressure increases from all sides, he finds himself longing for Tania Moretti, the Chief of Police's beautiful assistant - but matters become more complicated when a vengeful gangster starts hounding him. Drama, starring Rufus Sewell and Caterina Murino

programme.title: Death on the Tyne
programme.desc: Comedy mystery following on from Murder on the Blackpool Express, focusing this time on an eventful and bloody overnight ferry trip from Newcastle to Amsterdam. During the turbulent journey, the passengers and crew begin to get bumped off one by one in grizzly circumstances, and everyone seems to have a motive for murder. Johnny Vegas, Sian Gibson, Sheila Reid, Sue Johnston, Georgie Glen, James Fleet, Felicity Montagu and Doon Mackichan are among the all-star cast

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Burke and the team investigate university lecturer and crime novelist Mark Joffe, whose debut publication includes the detailed description of a murder identical to that of a real-life unsolved case. As the case unfolds, Burke becomes convinced of the writer's guilt, but cannot seem to find sufficient evidence to charge him. Guest starring Finlay Robertson (Life Begins), with Alex Norton, Blythe Duff and John Michie

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: A black man is murdered in a jail cell by a confirmed racist - a crime the prison service blames on overcrowding. However, the judge's probing reveals the institution wasn't operating at full capacity. A drink-driving appeal puts the bench at loggerheads, and Jo distances herself from her former lover. With Donald Sinden and Jenny Seagrove

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: Charlie is appointed to defend an animal rights activist accused of murdering an eminent scientist - a case that will be heard before her father. The defence barrister's support for her client's cause leads her to make a series of unprofessional outbursts in court, while Jo's relationship with Marc Thompson creates tension

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: The nerves set in as Sandra wakes for the first day of her new job, which sees her helping a man understand his poorly father's deteriorating condition. Craig visits the psychologist to find out if he's dyslexic, before sneaking off to a party in Bradford with Lucy. A poor showing on the rugby pitch forces Vic to consider a merger between the Scorpions and their similarly afflicted rivals, the Hoxton Giants

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Nate investigates a knife attack on young girl Aisha, only for the case to take on a personal note when he discovers his new love interest, nurse Maria Joseph, agreed not to report the injury when she treated the wounded girl in hospital. Further evidence reveals that Aisha may be the victim of a stalker - and when she is kidnapped, the race is on to rescue her from a potential killer

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Malick balances his new role as Clinical Skills Lead with his duties as a doctor, but starts to realise how serious a teacher's responsibilities can be. Greg struggles to remain professional when he gets too involved in a young patient's case, and Annalese visits Holby with some unexpected news for Michael. With Hannah Waterman (EastEnders' Laura Beale) and Murray Head (Heartbeat)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Part one of two. Doctors Ruth Winters and Toby De Silva join the emergency department and have very different experiences during their first day on the job. While the former gets off to a confident and stress-free start, the latter has an explosive introduction to his new duties when he treats people following a major incident. Matthew Needham and Georgia Taylor join the cast

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Max's Polish ancestry proves useful when he investigates an extortion racket in Sun Hill's frightened immigrant community. The case brings him into contact with the family of a man he shot dead during his time with the armed response unit. Honeysuckle Weeks guest stars

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian leaves for Italy where Cindy is hiding out. Joe wants to go home to Bolton. Diane leaves the Square - and leaves her son behind

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian and Ros are in Italy looking for Cindy and the boys. They are joined by Grant and Phil, who end up being arrested after a fight. Ros eventually finds Cindy in a nightclub

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Sarah attends a back-street doctor, but cannot go through with the operation. Carolyn gets offered a job as art critic for one of Sir Richard's Sydney newspapers

programme.title: Bergerac
programme.desc: The son of an eccentric millionaire suspects his father's housekeeper is plotting to murder the old boy for his money. Jersey-set police drama, starring John Nettles and Terence Alexander

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: Helen and Paul's romantic reverie is broken by the discovery of a car in a river with the body of its owner still at the wheel. It eventually emerges that the victim's wife was having an illicit affair with her own brother. Nigel Le Vaillant stars

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon tries to make the best of her job at the bakery and Dorien joins the Open University, while Tracey continues to study at the school of hard knocks. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Joe Maplin's plans for expansion cause chaos with the Yellowcoats when an old cottage is scheduled for demolition. Comedy, starring Paul Shane

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: Audrey takes a holiday, but frets about leaving her beloved Grantleigh Manor in DeVere's hands. Penelope Keith stars

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: In the hope of avoiding a fuss, Clegg plans a quiet birthday lunch at a nice hotel - but his pals are sure to throw a few spanners in the works. Long-running comedy set in Yorkshire, from the pen of Roy Clarke. Peter Sallis and Frank Thornton star

programme.title: The Coroner
programme.desc: A volunteer in the Lighthaven lifeboat crew drowns following a hoax call-out, and Jane suspects foul play, despite Davey's scepticism

programme.title: Luther
programme.desc: Two hoodies are shot in what appears to be a gangland crime, until a third murder victim gives Luther a vital clue - someone is killing people who have a criminal record. Sure enough the culprit soon posts a film of himself online, calling for changes to the public justice system. The race is on to find the vigilante before he strikes again - but with so many potential victims, that may be easier said than done. As ever, the DCI's personal life gets in the way of his investigation when a distraught Mary turns up after a visit from Stark and Gray - can he convince her to ever trust him again? Idris Elba and Sienna Guillory star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: DNA tests on a suspected robber prove his innocence but also reveal his father was an unnamed vagrant strangled on a tube train 15 years earlier. The team reopens the case, going in pursuit of the victim's other family members - including the man they believe to be his brother - while Brian questions the homeless community, only to fall foul of a gang of robbers. Peter Davison and Keith Allen guest star

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: The search for an abducted student leads nowhere until a 'psychic adviser' to the missing girl's mother approaches the police, offering to help track her down. Burke grudgingly agrees to attend one of her shows and is stunned by the accuracy of information he is given about his father that no-one else could possibly know. Drama, starring Alex Norton and Blythe Duff

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Boyd expects a confession of guilt when he is summoned to prison by one of the inmates, a former policeman serving life for the murder of his partner. The convict does make a revelation - but what he has to say causes the detective out to re-examine the facts of the old case from every possible angle to assure himself of the man's guilt. Trevor Eve and Felicite du Jeu star

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Gene investigates the shooting of a Ugandan Asian man and jumps to the conclusion that the attack is drug-related when he finds a suspicious bag of powder on the victim. When the substance turns out to be heroin, the policeman becomes determined to keep it off the streets of Manchester - and decides to do whatever it takes to get his way. Fantasy drama, starring Philip Glenister and John Simm

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Henry organises a team-building exercise in the Lake District for Goddard's staff - an adventure that nearly ends in tragedy. Jacqui continues to push Stephen away, going for a scan without even telling him, but an awkward encounter with Alison during a girls' night out changes her perspective. Drama, starring Pam Ferris and Sarah Lancashire

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Max's Polish ancestry proves useful when he investigates an extortion racket in Sun Hill's frightened immigrant community. programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Greg puts his career at risk when he is unable to distance himself from a patient, and is forced to confront uncomfortable truths from his past. Malick struggles to stay focused on the ward as he worries about a serious complaint made against him, while Michael makes plans to spend a day off with his daughter Jasmine - only for work to get in the way once again

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Part two of two. The aftermath of the explosion is seen from Charlie's perspective as he struggles to stop the body count rising even further. When the veteran nurse makes a simple mistake it is left to Connie Beauchamp to take charge and whip the overstretched team into shape. To make matters worse, the Emergency Department is then evacuated due to an unattended bag. Guest starring Holby City's Amanda Mealing

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Max sets out to find the teenage murder suspect who has gone on the run after being tipped off about the police sting by his stepmother. Stuart voices his concern that his colleague is letting personal involvement with the family cloud his judgement - an opinion borne out when Max confronts the armed youngster without telling anybody of his plans. Honeysuckle Weeks guest stars

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Peggy puts her foot down and Terry makes Tony face reality about his mother

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian tries to get Cindy into bed while Grant and Phil take Steven and Peter to the airport. They all meet up later and get on the plane as Cindy arrives to see them depart

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Olivia expresses her reservations about the artist Lloyd Ellis Parker to James, while Rene is distraught to learn about Sarah's pregnancy. Meanwhile, Carolyn confronts George about Regina, and Elizabeth has lunch with Douglas Goddard

programme.title: Bergerac
programme.desc: A notorious French gangster is at large, but the only information the detectives hold is that his girlfriend has boarded a flight to Jersey. Drama, guest starring Greta Scacchi, with John Nettles and Cecile Paoli

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: A press conference is called when a woman mysteriously disappears, but Dangerfield suspects her boyfriend is behind it. Drama, starring Nigel Le Vaillant

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: An activity weekend lands Dorien in hospital - where she is mortified that the private doctors might think she fraternises with the likes of Sharon and Tracey. Veteran TV weatherman Ian McCaskill appears as himself

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: The Yellowcoats fight to save Fred's horses from the glue factory - with unexpected results. Comedy, starring Felix Bowness

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: DeVere decides to shoot a commercial for his supermarket empire at Grantleigh and Audrey takes advantage of the cameras to tell the world what she thinks of him. Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: After seeing a man skiing on a van roof, the pals are intrigued to discover it is Gavin Hinchcliffe, an old friend renowned for his eccentricity. Comedy series, guest starring Bernard Cribbins, with Peter Sallis, Frank Thornton and Josephine Tewson

programme.title: The Last Detective
programme.desc: Comedy drama series from A Touch of Frost writer Richard Harris. Detective Constable 'Dangerous' Davies is an obstinate traditionalist used to being given bottom-of-the-pile jobs at his north London station until a routine inquiry offers him a lead in a 20-year-old unsolved murder case. Enlisting the help of his eccentric drinking buddy Mod Lewis, the mild-mannered detective sets out to prove himself to his colleagues. Peter Davison and Sean Hughes star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Fresh DNA tests on an unidentified body found in 1996 reveal a family link to a Home Office fingerprint analyst, who has not seen her brother since she was seven, when he brought her over to the UK from their home country of Albania. The victim had been working as a police interpreter - and the motive seems clear when it turns out his last assignment was translating for a witness in a murder trial against a notorious Albanian criminal. Meanwhile, Gerry embarks on a French cookery course, but the language barrier results in a rather surprising date. Amanda Redman and Dennis Waterman star

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Ross has his contact book stolen by a woman he brought home from the pub, and the next morning one of his informants is found murdered, with the word 'grass' daubed in red paint at the scene of the crime. The heat is on for the inspector as he realises he has to come clean about what happened - and the pressure increases when someone else mentioned in the book is found dead. John Michie stars

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A millionaire businessman is found dead a year after his disappearance. Evidence suggests he was imprisoned in a cellar immediately before his death, and it's not long before a second body is discovered at the scene. The other corpse has been dead for far longer - and it seems both murders could be the work of an unlikely suspect

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Sam and the rest of the CID unit get to work in an effort to prove Gene's innocence when he becomes a suspect in a murder case. But as the investigation continues, further evidence is stacked up against Hunt, leading to a conclusion that rocks the very foundations of the detective's already fragile world. John Simm and Philip Glenister star

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Italy v England (Kick-off 8.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the final, as the champions are crowned at Wembley Stadium. After a nervy semi-final appearance that saw them overcome a deficit for the first time during the tournament, Gareth Southgate's England players secured their place in the final of a major competition for just the second time in history. Their opponents have a rich pedigree, having won the World Cup four times and the European Championships once, and come into this match on the back of a 32-game unbeaten run. With analysis from Alan Shearer, Rio Ferdinand and Jurgen Klinsmann

programme.title: Crouchy's Year Late Euros: Live
programme.desc: Former England striker Peter Crouch gives his unique take on the latest Euro 2020 action alongside Maya Jama, with music from Alex Horne and the Horne Section

programme.title: Krept and Konan: We Are England
programme.desc: Documentary following the rap duo as they set out to make the England anthem for the Euro 2020 tournament. Along the way they get to know the squad, meeting players including Everton's Dominic Calvert-Lewin, Aston Villa's Tyrone Mings and West Ham's Declan Rice, and ask manager Gareth Southgate which ingredients he would like in the final song. They also seek advice from fellow artists Aitch and Big Zuu

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Italy v England. Another chance to see the showpiece match, as the tournament concluded at Wembley Stadium

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit two neighbouring cottages in Derbyshire, a terrace in Bolton and a semi in Leeds before they are sold under the hammer

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass look back at their investigation into the case of two men sentenced to death for the murder of a Liverpool woman in 1951

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Further action from the UK's most remote hospital. A carrot cake causes an emergency for a nut allergy sufferer who's airlifted to Shetland

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents the show from Wrexham, and discovers the story of The Ladies of Llangollen. Roo Irvine and Philip Serrell provide expert advice to today's teams

programme.title: WPC 56
programme.desc: Drama centred on the fortunes of police officer Gina Dawson, who faces an uphill battle to carve out a successful career for herself in the male-dominated world of the 1950s. Brinford Police Station is rocked by a shocking discovery in an abandoned mine, while two senior officers investigate an attack on a young woman. Jennie Jacques and John Light star

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and brothers David and Harry Rich brothers reveal their favourite makeovers and also offer gardening tips and ideas. The first edition features a selection of family gardens, including a weird and wonderful Alice in Wonderland-inspired makeover, and one with a basketball court created for two children with mobility issues

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple who want to move from Surrey to Devon, hoping to find a sizeable home to fit three generations of their family and all for a budget of £800,000

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Master craftsman Simon Parfett and his team help people with a little land or a beautiful garden generate extra income by creating their own unique B&Bs. They begin by heading to the picturesque Wye Valley to help George and Al transform their battered old family camper van

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charlie Ross and Izzie Balmer start their hunt in Stow-on-the-Wold, Gloucestershire, buying items including a military first-aid kit and a toast rack to sell at auction in Cardiff

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: BBC correspondent Lucy Adams is one of more than a million people in the UK with Long Covid. In his programme, she reports on what the future holds for sufferers of long Covid symptoms, talking to patients, doctors with increasing caseloads, and scientists carrying out research into the condition

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chelsea and Gray cause havoc at his work function when they make the most of the free drinks, embarrassing themselves in front of Gray's boss Laura. Honey reaches out to a grief-stricken Suki, Sheree gives Lola a word of warning and Jay offers Billy an olive branch

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich meet Liz, Geoff and their daughter Lois in Yate, Gloucestershire, where the hope is they can use a £4,500 budget to create a garden with a nautical theme in memory of Liz's father, who was known locally as ''The Pirate'

programme.title: Gold Rush: Our Race to Olympic Glory
programme.desc: Britain makes its bid to host the 2012 games, while the British team goes in search of new talent, including a nine-year-old Tom Daley beginning his sporting career. Christine Ohuruogu, the potential face of the 2012 games, discusses her dramatic fall from grace after missing three drugs tests, and her subsequent return to the track. The episode concludes with the 2008 games in Beijing, where Britain turned its fortunes around with a haul of 51 medals

programme.title: High: Confessions of an Ibiza Drug Mule
programme.desc: Michaella discusses how she claimed to have been forced into smuggling in the hope of getting a reduced sentence, only to find herself spending the first of what would be many days behind bars in the cells of the police's anti-narcotics division. However, her story soon caught the attention of the international press, with reporters around the world desperate for the inside scoop

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Jennifer Saunders hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with Matt Forde and Ayesha Hazarika joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: A couple from Manchester answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The redevelopment of a million-pound-plus house in Fulham, as well as the progress of auction buys in St Helens near Liverpool and Stoke in Staffordshire

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein catch up on the investigation into the 1937 murder of 23-year-old Ruby Keen, who was found strangled to death with her own scarf in an alleyway in Leighton Buzzard, Bedfordshire

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the Gilbert Bain Hospital in Lerwick, following the work of those providing emergency and medical care to the Shetland Islands' 23,000 residents

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Jonathan Pratt and Nick Hall help two teams find items at Peterborough's Festival of Antiques, while presenter Eric Knowles visits the Coffin Works museum in Birmingham

programme.title: WPC 56
programme.desc: Gina risks her career to prove Donald is innocent when a woman is attacked in Victoria Park. Meanwhile, Jack uncovers important information in connection with the case of the missing boys, while Pratt experiences problems with a boy of his own. Police drama set in the 1950s, starring Jennie Jacques

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: The team recalls some of their favourite quirky garden rescues, including a music festival-inspired plot featuring an air-raid shelter, and a fusion of Yorkshire and Japan

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a Kent family find a new home in the East Riding of Yorkshire with room for a catering business, and takes a trip on a traditional fishing boat in Bridlington

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon Parfett and the team are in Herefordshire to meet new parents Cheryl and Bill and help get their chalet on a hill from flat-pack to Scandinavian chic

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antiques experts Charlie Ross and Izzie Balmer head to Surrey, where Izzie finds a piece of jewellery that causes quite a stir at auction, and Charlie uncovers a rare table

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas presents another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Patrick tries to get through to an increasingly agitated Isaac, asking him where Ruby and Martin's children are and promising that he will not let the police hurt him. Peter is upset at the turn of events with Ash so Kathy tells him a few home truths, Chelsea lands herself in a sticky situation at Gray's house and Tommy's skills are tested at the football trial

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Dom is asked to give a keynote speech at Jeni's fundraiser, but his elation is short-lived when mum Carole comes in with a burn on her arm. Is she hiding something? The cat is out of the bag when Kylie blurts out Ange's secret to Josh, while Max desperately tries to rally the troops after the disastrous findings of the Care Quality Commission

programme.title: Gravity (2013)
programme.desc: Two astronauts are left stranded in orbit above the Earth when the debris from an exploding satellite destroys their shuttle. Left drifting in space with no way to contact mission control, they begin a perilous journey to reach the safety of a space station before their oxygen supply runs out. Thriller, starring Sandra Bullock and George Clooney

programme.title: High: Confessions of an Ibiza Drug Mule
programme.desc: Michaella explains she was faced with a dilemma - stick to her story about being forced to carry the drugs and face 15 years in jail, or come clean and break her mother's heart

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman tries to keep up appearances as he makes a troubling discovery, while Dylan and Emma welcome an unexpected guest

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman receives a gift from Chick, but finds his date does not go according to plan. Romero's frustrations reach a tipping point

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: Chris Bavin meets the Clovelly Bloomers, who are determined to win gold despite having to garden in the extremely steep landscape of their ancient Devon fishing village

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don catches up with all his summer jobs in the border and the vegetable garden. Carol Klein gets inspiration for planting combinations at Cothay Manor and Gardens, and Arit Anderson learns about slugs and snails

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison celebrate the 150th anniversary of Wimbledon Common, encountering the horseback patrols that enforce the Common's 57 bylaws and discovering how the Wombles have inspired a whole new generation of ecologists. Tom Heap investigates whether efforts to protect the world's oceans are delivering for UK waters, and Dwayne Fields leads four youngsters at a crossroad in their lives on an expedition into the Welsh wilderness

programme.title: Weatherman Walking: The Welsh Coast
programme.desc: Derek Brockway explores the dramatic landscape of the north Gower saltmarshes, where he learns of the magical and mystical fairy folk, helps a sheep farmer round up his flock and even has a go at weaving. Plus, marine biologist Lizzie Daly tracks trout and Matt Bassett rides the waves on a new 'adaptive' surfboard at Caswell Bay

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Carole Baxter and Mairi Rattray take a look at the now blooming lovely wildflower bank that Chris Beardshaw cleared and weeded and sowed in 2019. Kirsty Wilson is in her pretty and productive allotment, repurposing an old wine box to make into a trendy planter for some of her purple produce

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights from throughout the series, offering viewers a chance to revisit some of their favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day 13 of the Grand Slam tournament at the All England Club, featuring the remaining wheelchair finals

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britain's baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones visits Cheddar Gorge in Somerset to hear the origins of the hymn Rock of Ages, while Gemma Hunt meets worship leader Martin Smith and new Christian group Bright City

programme.title: The Importance of Being Earnest (2002)
programme.desc: Two bachelors using the same pseudonym capture the hearts of a couple of girls during a delightful weekend in the countryside. However, their cunning deception leads to all manner of hilarious confusion as the ladies in question attempt to work out who is who. Adaptation of Oscar Wilde's comedy, starring Rupert Everett, Colin Firth, Frances O'Connor, Reese Witherspoon, Judi Dench and Tom Wilkinson

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The show comes from the Old Royal Naval College in Greenwich, where finds include a collection of fans and a signed postcard. Paul Martin takes a tour of the Cutty Sark

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: In the Highlands, new entrant crofters Lynn and Sandra welcome more new sheep and take delivery of a batch of hens that they hope will lay the foundations of an 'egg empire'. In Northern Ireland, the Rodgers host a barbecue for the local Young Farmers Club, but it is no ordinary flame-grilled affair, while Raymond and Johnny gather the flock to wean this year's lambs in the Cairngorms

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Tom Heap and Margherita Taylor look at the impact of canals on the history of the British countryside. They the Grand Union Canal and celebrating the 200th anniversary of the Regent's Canal, a once-commercial route that now brings a slice of countryside to the city, benefitting both residents and wildlife. Plus, wildlife cameraman Jack Perks documents some frogs, and an update on the fortunes of a family staking their future on the success of high-tech farming

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents further live coverage on day 13 of the Grand Slam tournament at the All England Club, where the concluding doubles finals are set to take place. Hsieh Su-wei and Barbora Strycova concluded the tournament's Centre Court proceedings two years ago with a 6-2, 6-4 ladies' doubles triumph over Gabriela Dabrowski and Xu Yifan. Over on the No.1 Court, Ivan Dodig and Latisha Chan prevailed 6-2, 6-3 over Robert Lindstedt and Jelena Ostapenko in the mixed doubles

programme.title: Our Wild Adventures
programme.desc: BBC wildlife presenters and crew reflect on memorable visits to India, Bhutan and Bangladesh, with Liz Bonnin remembering a remarkable morning migration by thousands of cranes to the small village of Khichan in Rajasthan. Chris Packham looking back on a tiger-spotting trip, while Steve Backshall and Gordon Buchanan recall an expedition looking for tigers in the mountains of Bhutan

programme.title: The Duchess (2008)
programme.desc: Fact-based period drama, chronicling the life of Georgiana Spencer who became Duchess of Devonshire in 1774 at the age of 17. Adored by the public for her extravagant lifestyle, Spencer endured a troubled marriage and was eventually driven into the arms of Charles Grey, a senior Whig politician. Starring Keira Knightley, Ralph Fiennes, Dominic Cooper and Charlotte Rampling

programme.title: Today at Wimbledon
programme.desc: Clare Balding and guests look back at highlights of the men's singles final, where the third Grand Slam title of the year was set to be decided. Plus, news from the rest of the day's finals as the famous tournament was brought to a conclusion, and a review of this year's staging of the event

programme.title: Golf: The Scottish Open
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents action from day four of this year's tournament, which took place at The Renaissance Club in North Berwick. England's Aaron Rai was the victorious player here in 2020

programme.title: Every Day (2018)
programme.desc: Sixteen-year-old Rhiannon falls in love with a mysterious soul named A, who inhabits a different body every day. The two work to find one another daily, not knowing what or who the next 24 hours will bring. Romantic fantasy drama, starring Angourie Rice and Justice Smith

programme.title: Bad Influencer: The Great Insta Con
programme.desc: The rise and fall of internet wellness guru Belle Gibson, who cultivated an adoring online following with the story that she had successfully treated her own cancer by eating a plant-based diet and using alternative natural therapies. There was one major problem with Belle's story - she never had cancer

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Given the hospital's chequered history, everyone is anxious and on high alert as the Care Quality Commission conducts an emergency inspection. Josh's mother puts pressure on his age-gap romance with Ange, while doubt is cast on a patient Rich Peterson's claim to have been bitten by a dog

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reexamine a case from 1882, when two boys making their way home from school saw two men in distinctive long black coats shoot Co Kerry farmer and landowner Thomas Browne dead. Despite no murder weapon being recovered, unemployed tenant farmer Sylvester Poff and his cousin James Barrett were arrested, charged and later found guilty of Browne's murder

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion takes a look at the rarest and most unique items bought by past contestants and also finds out about a rare book on birds worth millions of pounds. Plus, Natasha Raskin Sharp talks to Charles Hanson about Faberge flowers, and Charlie Ross explores a unique collection of self-playing instruments

programme.title: Brian Cox's Adventures in Space and Time
programme.desc: The physicist takes a fresh look at the concept of gravity, revealing it to be far more than just the force that makes things fall to the ground. He revisits his previous programmes, including being first on the scene to meet a capsule returning three astronauts from the International Space Station, and demonstrating how a bowling ball and feather fall at the same speed in the largest vacuum chamber in the world

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Mel Giedroyc in which pairs of contestants go head-to-head to crack a series of passwords as they attempt to take home the £2,500 prize money

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Contestants from Bolton and Leeds take part in the antiques challenge, presented by Paul Martin. The pair are shown the tricks of the trade before an auction showdown

programme.title: Rise of the Continents
programme.desc: Geologist Professor Iain Stewart examines key moments in the development of the world's major land masses. He begins by looking at how Africa was formed from the wreckage of a long-lost supercontinent, with its landmarks, mineral wealth and wildlife providing clues that help piece together the story of its creation. He also reveals how this deep history has left its mark on modern day Africa and the world

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The first of the Central region heats sees returning contender Pip Lacey and newcomers Ryan Simpson and Nick Deverell-Smith preparing their starters. They must impress a Great British Menu veteran if they are to cook for the judges in the regional final

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three Central region chefs fight it out for the fish course, with Pip Lacey combining lobster with strawberry in tribute to the classic Wimbledon strawberries and cream. Ryan Simpson also uses lobster in his recreation of a barbecue and has invented a special technique to create his unique chips, while Nick Deverell-Smith focuses on crab to make a savoury cream tea

programme.title: The Bear Family and Me
programme.desc: Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan follows a family of wild black bears for a year. He arrives in the forests of Minnesota in spring to observe the animals emerging from their dens. However, before he can start filming, he faces the challenge of earning their trust and overcoming his own fear of the creatures, which have a dangerous reputation

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Blackburn Cathedral, Charles Hanson values a rare Eureka clock and Anita Manning is drawn to four oil paintings of the Ribble Valley. Host Paul Martin meets up with Michelin-star chef Nigel Haworth to discover how to make the perfect Lancashire hotpot

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty, Jordan Stephens, Sally Lindsay and David O'Doherty test their general knowledge in a variety of trivia-based games across the week, with a winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble return to Longleat just as the Covid-19 pandemic forces the park to close, while the keepers set about keeping the animals safe, fed and entertained

programme.title: Gold Town
programme.desc: Following people working for a mining company as they attempt to extract gold from a vein in Beinn Chuirn, a hill near the village of Tyndrum at the northern extremity of Loch Lomond and the Trossachs National Park. The endeavour could bring valuable employment to the local community, but the conditions are challenging

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell returns with the quiz based on patience and lateral thinking, with the opening match seeing the Scrubs taking on the Librarians. They compete to draw together the connections between things that at first glance seem utterly random, with one set of clues comprising Crime in France, Naked in Germany, Gazier in Italy, and Agarics in Spain

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman presides as the academic quiz returns for its 28th series since the revival of the show in 1995, with the opening match of the first round seeing four students from King's College London take on a quartet from the University of Glasgow

programme.title: Amol Rajan Interviews
programme.desc: The BBC's media editor and Radio 4 Today presenter sits down with pioneers, leaders and maverick thinkers working in politics, technology, business and culture who are shaping the rapidly changing 21st-century world. In the first edition, he talks to Indian-American business executive Sundar Pichai, the CEO of Alphabet Inc and its subsidiary Google, on issues from the company's hotly debated privacy practices and tax policies to its developments in AI and quantum computing.

programme.title: The Big Asian Stand-Up
programme.desc: From the City Varieties in Leeds, Nish Kumar hosts the first of two programmes showcasing a new wave of Asian comedic talent from the UK and beyond as part of a celebration of the cultural and artistic contribution of Asian Britons. Award-winning comedians Tez Ilyas and Emily Lloyd-Saini feature, alongside Indian stand-up Anuvab Pal, Bradford-based comic Isma Almas and rising star Eshaan Akbar

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Reggie in China
programme.desc: Reggie Yates travels to China and visits four very different cities. His first stop is Shenzhen, which over the past 40 years has grown from a region dominated by fishing and farmland to a bustling metropolis that is home to 12 million people and at the centre of the country's technological revolution. He meets the inventors of an earpiece that uses algorithms to translate speech into other languages, discovers more about the differing attitudes between generations, and witnesses a demonstration of a flying motorcycle

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison celebrate the 150th anniversary of Wimbledon Common, encountering the horseback patrols that enforce the Common's 57 bylaws and discovering how the Wombles have inspired a whole new generation of ecologists. Tom Heap investigates whether efforts to protect the world's oceans are delivering for UK waters, and Dwayne Fields leads four youngsters at a crossroad in their lives on an expedition into the Welsh wilderness

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Luke and Carl visit a classic car workshop in search of the biggest debt they have ever come across - owed to a man whose lifelong dream of owning a vintage Jaguar was shattered

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: After an elderly couple's home is broken into, a police officer looks at burglary patterns in the area and predicts where and when the culprits are likely to strike again. Only this time, he's waiting for them

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass look back at their investigation into the case of two men sentenced to death for the murder of a Liverpool woman in 1951

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents the show from Wrexham, and discovers the story of The Ladies of Llangollen. Roo Irvine and Philip Serrell provide expert advice to today's teams

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Adam Frost adds tall plants for shady conditions to his borders, shows how to make a window box for growing vegetables and herbs and gives tips on how to grow plants successfully in peat-free compost. Nick Bailey visits Kew Gardens to explore their collection of ferns and cameras return to the urban garden of Kate Bradbury to find out how she ensures that visiting wildlife is kept well fed throughout the summer months. Plus, the Isle of Man garden where a range of exotic plants are thriving in the microclimate on the island and a nurseryman in Kent with a passion for hydrangeas

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the latest fixtures, including Leeds Rhinos v Catalans Dragons, Castleford Tigers v Salford Red Devils and Wakefield Trinity v St Helens

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Northamptonshire is the location for the antiques challenge, as Paul Martin teaches new recruits Dennie Smith and Marria Akhtar the tricks of the trade before an auction showdown

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: In Bristol, Paul Martin teaches would-be antiques dealers the tricks of the trade, pitching Roger Holbrook against Shahir Farnood. An auction showdown later reveals who has made the largest profit and made the highest percentage of profit

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The entrants representing the Central regions, Pip Lacey, Ryan Simpson and Nick Deverell-Smith, go all out to impress with their main courses. Ryan hopes to elevate a ploughman's lunch into a banquet-worthy dish, while Pip pays tribute to an unusual Wimbledon icon, and Nick celebrates venison in his main course

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Central chefs - Pip Lacey, Nick Deverell-Smith and Ryan Simpson - prepare their desserts, knowing this is the last chance to impress before one of them has to leave the competition. Two of them use pineapple, in reference to the fruit on top of the Wimbledon men's singles trophy, while the third celebrates the summer ice-cream van. Which two will make it through to tomorrow?

programme.title: The Bear Family and Me
programme.desc: Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan returns to Minnesota in the summer to catch up with the black bears, but is upset to discover that cub Hope has been abandoned by her mother. With the young animal facing starvation, he is forced to step in and become a surrogate parent, and balances his new duties with continued study of the rest of the family

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antique experts Catherine Southon and Jonathan Pratt search for items in Kent to take off to auction. Catherine falls for a colourful brooch and Jonathan unearths a horn beaker used by Winston Churchill. Host Paul Martin uncovers the story of Dover Castle's secret tunnels.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Richard Osman hosts as BBC Breakfast's Naga Munchetty, singer Jordan Stephens, actor Sally Lindsay and comedian David O'Doherty test their general knowledge

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents live coverage of the Diamond League meeting from Gateshead International Stadium, as the athletes involved fine-tune preparations for the Tokyo Olympics. British world champions Dina Asher-Smith and Katarina Johnson-Thompson are among those set to be involved, along with Ethiopia's Sifan Hassan who will be competing over the mile distance that she holds the world record in

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: Forensic scene investigator Drew Birtles searches for evidence when a woman in her 50s alleges that an engineer working in her home indecently exposed himself. Facing a case that is difficult to prove, Drew sets to work using forensic light sources and DNA evidence to build a picture of what went on in the woman's house.

programme.title: Cunk on Britain
programme.desc: Philomena Cunk continues her historical odyssey, exploring the stories of the Tudors, the Georgians, the English Civil War, William Shakespeare, Witchfinder General Matthew Hopkins and Lord Horatio Nelson. She also looks into Henry VIII's chronic addiction to wives. With historian Kate Williams

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events

programme.title: ODI Cricket Highlights
programme.desc: England v Pakistan. Action from the final one-day international in the three-match series, which took place at Edgbaston

programme.title: Gods of Snooker
programme.desc: The sport's rise to prominence, beginning by examining how Alex 'Hurricane' Higgins upset the snooker establishment to help transform a game played in the backrooms of working men's clubs into a TV ratings hit. Interest in the sport had been growing thanks to the new possibilities of colour broadcasting, and in particular the weekly snooker show Pot Black, but it was the antics of the unpredictable Ulstersman that took the game stratospheric

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reexamine the case of an 18-year-old hotel employee who was accused of murdering a titled guest in 1922

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: How a brave mother fought back against car thieves as they tried to steal her car from her driveway - with her baby inside. Narrated by Rav Wilding

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Sacred Music: The Story of Allegri's Miserere
programme.desc: Simon Russell Beale explores the 300-year history of the sacred choral work, which was decreed by the Pope to be too beautiful to be heard outside the Sistine Chapel. An English organist, a choirmaster from Worcester and the great composers Mozart and Mendelssohn all played a part in the transformation of Allegri's original 17th-century composition into the haunting music known today. Featuring a performance of the piece by award-winning choir the Sixteen, conducted by Harry Christophers

programme.title: Vienna Summer Night Concert 2021
programme.desc: Summer-night concert from the grounds of the Schoenbrunn Palace in Vienna

programme.title: World War Two: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Laurence Rees examines the clash of values between Josef Stalin and Winston Churchill and how they were played out over the future of Poland in 1944. Records of secret meetings reveal the British prime minister thought he could persuade the Soviets to allow an independent government in the country the UK had gone into the war to protect, but the efforts to play politics threatened the integrity of the alliance

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: Maggie Aderin-Pocock and Chris Lintott discover how the search for extra-terrestrial life in the universe has been reported by the BBC over the past six decades, from the birth of radio astronomy in the 1960s to the discovery of the first planets outside of our own solar system in the 1990s, and right up to probes exploring our neighbouring planets in the present day

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: The scientists scouring the universe for signs of extraterrestrial life, including the film researchers at the Green Bank Telescope searching for radio signals from 'Tabby's Star', a star so mysterious some scientists believe it might be surrounded by a Dyson Sphere - a vast energy collector built by advanced aliens. The film also features the 2006 discovery by husband and wife team Duncan Lorimer and Maura McLaughlin of an enigmatic signal from space, known as a fast radio burst, and its subsequent explanations

programme.title: The Chauffeur's Tale
programme.desc: With millions of fans worldwide, and a career spanning more than seven decades, Barbara Cartland was not only a record-breaking novelist, but also a colourful public figure in her own right. Robert Alderson, former chauffeur and butler to the writer, discusses how she maintained the sheen on her own glossy character

programme.title: Missions
programme.desc: Sci-fi drama about the first manned mission to Mars, which faces a major crisis just as the crew are about to land, threatening to slit an already fractious crew. In French and Russian

programme.title: Missions
programme.desc: Sci-fi drama. A sub-team seeks salvage to save the stricken craft, but the trio's discovery of a body means a surprisingly harsh reception on their return. In French and Russian

programme.title: Vienna Summer Night Concert 2021
programme.desc: Summer-night concert from the grounds of the Schoenbrunn Palace in Vienna

programme.title: Sacred Music: The Story of Allegri's Miserere
programme.desc: Simon Russell Beale explores the 300-year history of the sacred choral work, which was decreed by the Pope to be too beautiful to be heard outside the Sistine Chapel. An English organist, a choirmaster from Worcester and the great composers Mozart and Mendelssohn all played a part in the transformation of Allegri's original 17th-century composition into the haunting music known today. Featuring a performance of the piece by award-winning choir the Sixteen, conducted by Harry Christophers

programme.title: Return to The Cruise
programme.desc: Retrospective on the hit 1990s documentary, with Jane McDonald and the rest of the crew reminiscing about their maiden voyage aboard the luxury liner

programme.title: Art of Persia
programme.desc: Samira Ahmed goes back to when Persia faced its gravest threat - Genghis Khan - and reveals how the Safavids, who succeeded the Mongols, give Iran a powerful new identity

programme.title: Private Life of a Masterpiece
programme.desc: An exploration of Rodin's sculpture The Kiss. Considered by many to be the most sensual work of art in the last 200 years, the piece was highly controversial when first unveiled. Historians attempt to discover the identity of the models behind the intertwined lovers, and there's a look at the extraordinary events surrounding the statue

programme.title: Romancing the Stone: The Golden Ages of British Sculpture
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke examines the three 'golden ages' of British sculpture and the technical breakthroughs that made them possible. He begins with a look at the Norman ecclesiastical building programme and the masterworks created by anonymous medieval masons and artisans to the glory of God - until Henry VIII's Reformation brought wholesale destruction

programme.title: Fig Leaf: The Biggest Cover Up in History
programme.desc: Writer and broadcaster Stephen Smith explores the history of the fig leaf's use throughout 2,000 years of Western art and ethics. He explains how the work of Michelangelo fuelled the Fig Leaf Campaign censorship effort, how Bernini created a new form of erotica with his use of the slipping gauze, and how Rodin brought depictions of nudity back to the public's attention. The presenter argues that Victorians had a more sophisticated and mature attitude to sexuality than is found today, and offers his view that modern art could be redeemed from cheap sensation by the return of the fig leaf

programme.title: Missions
programme.desc: The rescued man is a reminder to Jeanne of her father's interest in space, and Mars in particular. In French

programme.title: Missions

programme.desc: The documentary about the American writer reveals how, having achieved a level of fame rarely seen in the literary world, Hemingway settled in Key West with Pauline Pfeiffer. He now spent his time seeking out new conquests in the natural world - marlin fishing in the Gulf Stream and hunting in Africa. With readings by Meryl Streep, Patricia Clarkson, Keri Russell, Joe Morton and Josh Lucas

programme.title: Michael Palin's Hemingway Adventure
programme.desc: The journey progresses to Key West to witness the 18th Annual Papa Hemingway lookalike contest, then to Uganda, where the novelist survived two air crashes in just 48 hours. Michael then journeys up the Nile to witness Murchison Falls and makes his way to Venice for the carnival

programme.title: Eugenics: Science's Greatest Scandal
programme.desc: Part one of two. Science journalist Angela Saini and disability rights activist Adam Pearson, reveal that eugenics - the controversial idea that was a driving force behind the Nazi death camps - originated in the upper echelons of the British scientific community. They also examine its legacy and continued influence today

programme.title: Apple Tree House
programme.desc: Aamir Tai, Miranda Sarfo Peprah, Summer Jenkins, William Vanderpuye, Shaheen Khan, Yasmeen Khan. Preschool drama series about children living on an extraordinary inner-city estate. Mali's dad, Nadeem, has brought Grandma Zainab tickets to watch the cricket - live! When Mali tells his gran that he doesn't want to go and watch the match because 'cricket's boring', he doesn't realise how much he's upset her. Mali is determined to go to Alex's football party instead. It's not until Mali really listens to his friends and some of the adults that he works out what might be the right thing to do to show his gran how much he cares about her.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog

programme.title: Garden Tales
programme.desc: Lawrie the Little Owl likes the quiet life. He has no time for adventures and no longing to explore anywhere outside of the garden. But Lawrie’s world gets turned upside down when Parker the Pheasant mistakenly overhears Lawrie reciting a poem and assumes he’s a famous explorer. Rumour spreads around the garden, and it’s not long before everyone is rushing to get a glimpse of this most famous of owls. It’s not the perfect quiet morning that poor old Lawrie had in mind.

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join 13-month-old Gia and twins, Chester and Elias, as they discover the world around them and find out what words they are learning. Both families are playing at home, talking about colours and a four-legged friend. They all head to baby classes, where they use music to practice their words, and some puppets come along to help them learn! Back at home, Chester and Elias play in the garden with their brother, going up and down the slide. Gia takes mum and granddad for a walk in the woods, where they see lots of beautiful things - they even find a bird! Finally, Chester and Elias play a counting game and Gia makes a wonderful word wall in the garden using pictures of things they saw that day. Remember, every child develops differently.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Nigel and Baby Bear welcome babies Sullivan, Araiya, River, Taiba, Luna and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Nigel has got a shopping basket inside his bag. All the parents and babies have different objects and toys that are themed around a trip to the shops too, which are explored through play, songs and a story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to hide a baby-appropriate shopping-themed toy or object inside a fabric bag or pillowcase, to discover with their baby! Some viewers at home show how they’ve been exploring shopping too. The fun children’s song Shopping Day features, as does a story about Baby Bear at the shops. The Baby Bear Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory lights and the Goodbye Song.

programme.title: Magic Hands

programme.title: Colours
programme.desc: Series for young children teaching them about colour. In this episode, we learn about objects associated with the colour green, from grass to grapes. We explore the meaning of being green, and the episode concludes with a specially written poem summarising the colour green.

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie visits a robot workshop where they design farming robots that help farmers grow crops. She meets three farming robots, Tom, Wilma and Richard that work together on the farm to help find the weeds in the fields. Maddie learns that Tom, a scanning robot, can see things we cannot see, using special cameras to scan and take photos of the weeds, animals, insects and crops in the field. Maddie then discovers how Tom sends pictures and information to an artificial intelligence computer called Wilma to build a map of the field. Finally, Maddie meets another Richard, a robot who uses the map to find and destroy the weeds. Maddie then visits a printing workshop to see how a 3D printer makes things. At the workshop, they design glasses frames to wear and Maddie uses her special camera to see how the 3D printing works by building up layers stacked on top of each other.

Bob becomes flustered at the thought of all the fuss, so Ruby suggests they go to the allotment to help pass the time. She promises they’ll be back in time for the big announcement. At the allotment, Ruby finds a white feather and makes a wish, but she won’t tell Magnus what it is. Magnus keeps trying to guess what Ruby’s wish is. Back at the Big Hub, Moira and Mrs Juniper are busy getting prepared, Moira wants everything to be perfect. Bob, Ruby and Magnus get so carried away with making a home for a caterpillar at the allotment that it’s after one o’clock by the time they realise the time. Oh no, they’ve missed the big announcement! Will Bob win the Green Hero Award? And will Magnus ever find out what Ruby wished for?
