programme.desc: The bunny helps do the shopping at Padget's shop, and cannot resist putting a strawberry lolly in his pocket while Flop is paying. The pair must then decide what to do

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The friends make a lot of noise in the kitchen - and they seem to be getting louder and louder. On The Big Red Boat, the Twirlywoo Screen entertains with some noisy dogs

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin has lots of summer fun with his friends, and Mr Tumble judges the Best Kept Garden

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dipsy and Laa-Laa trot and gallop across Teletubbyland pretending to be horses. Children in Tummy Tales help to groom and feed a horse

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The squirrels and Duggee go camping, but finding somewhere to pitch their tent proves more difficult that they expected

programme.title: School of Roars
programme.desc: The monsters celebrate Founders’ Day, but Icklewoo doesn’t know how she can take part.

programme.title: B.O.T and the Beasties
programme.desc: The Wonky Flier wants to fly up to the stars. Unfortunately, the small propeller on its head is not up to the job. Can B.O.T. help the beastie get airborne and complete the mission at the same time?

programme.title: Andy's Baby Animals
programme.desc: Andy Day learns about baby animals that are great at teamwork, including young meerkats in Africa, and Emperor penguins in the Antarctic

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk through the town to get to the allotment to pick some vegetables. On the way they cheer up a sad lamp post, count loaves of bread in the baker’s window, jump in muddy puddles and even find a golden chicken!

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Pink bird dons a feathered cap and plays a magical musical pipe, while Aimee and Ashley head to the village of Hamelin

programme.title: Tik Tak
programme.desc: Explore a magic rabbit toy and a puzzle of a tricycle, and watch Book Girl make a tractor.

programme.title: Let's Play
programme.desc: Sid Sloane steps through the Magic Curtain to become a painter and decorator, and is employed to help a team transform one of the biggest rooms in Lord Poshington's huge home

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: When the Olobobs go out for a picnic, big clouds cover the sky and it starts raining

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Glitch goes on a desert adventure to uncover a lost city, but is disappointed to learn someone else has found it already

programme.title: Andy's Prehistoric Adventures
programme.desc: Andy Day travels back through time to Mongolia 30 million years in the distant past, where he encounters the largest mammal to ever walk the Earth

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: AJ takes Evie and Anaya swimming, but Waffle must stay at home with Simon

programme.title: Old Jack's Boat
programme.desc: The village of Staithes is lit up at night by some naughty shooting stars

programme.title: Woolly & Tig
programme.desc: The spider shows Tig how sharing can be fun when her friend comes round to play

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say 'bus' and embarks on a whirlwind word adventure

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: A new superhero arrives in Alphaland in an edition that introduces compound words such as sheepdog, starfish and lunchbox

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: The big squares set out to land a square on the moon using square power alone. Learn about big square numbers with the Numberblocks.

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Dog and Pug meet an elephant and a mongoose who cannot play together.

programme.title: Messy Goes to OKIDO
programme.desc: On the day of Mayor Oki's birthday picnic, Messy and friends get into trouble with Zam's shrinkasizer

programme.title: Colours
programme.desc: Series for young children teaching them about colour. In this episode, we learn about objects that are associated with the colour white, from snow to space shuttles, and explore how the colour white is used. The episode concludes with a specially written poem summarising the colour white.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin goes shoe shopping with his friend

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies do running races and they each have a chance to win. In Tummy Tales, two sisters watch their father running in a race

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Pando insists on one last game of hide and seek and finds a great hiding place under Bing's bedcovers, but by the time the bunny finds him he is fast asleep

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Glitch gets into a mountain of trouble, as the Go Jetters attempt to climb the Matterhorn

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Harriet helps Timmy find a painted egg in a treasure hunt

programme.title: Ferne and Rory's Vet Tales
programme.desc: Rory meets Sophia whose cat, Oscar, is getting a check-up at the vets before trying a new treatment, while Ferne visits a zoo vet who is checking on their arctic fox

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Captain HeyHo's coat has a nasty smell, so Cook and Line try to help her out

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The squirrels set off for a jamboree to make some new friends, play games and have a picnic. They encounter another group of young animals with their very own version of Duggee, but everything they do is different - even Roly finds one of them too loud

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Peter is forced to come to the rescue when Flopsy and Mopsy get into trouble with Old Brown, but he could end up losing his previous journal in the process

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. He works there with his boss, Mr Hammond and tech wizard Jen

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters arrive at the Bonneville Salt Flats in Utah, USA, a huge dry lakebed that is perfect for high-speed driving

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Molly examines her shelf of special things at the back of Mack’s Stall. She chooses the home-made snail and remembers the day she got it. Mack has finished repairing an old toy dog of Daisy’s as a surprise. An impatient Molly asks him to phone Daisy right away. At the Vets, a stressed Daisy answers and tells Mack she’s too busy at the moment. Feeling ignored, Mack goes for a walk. Molly decides to get involved, texting Daisy from Mack’s phone to say that a dog urgently needs her help at the Hub. If Daisy can see the trouble Mack has gone to with her childhood toy, they’ll surely make up again. Meanwhile, Bob, Magnus and Suki discover a stowaway in one of Bob’s plant pots – ‘Snailzy’ the snail. Magnus and Suki take him back to the Kids Club, but disagree on how to look after him best. Later, Daisy arrives at the Hub. Mack is nowhere to be seen and there is no ‘dog emergency’. She heads back to the Vets, even more frustrated than before. Realising her mistake, Molly confesses to James. T

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle ends up in Evie's class, when he must be still so the headteacher doesn't see him.

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo & Gran Gran have their own sports day, including an egg and spoon race.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Sarah's tuba is making strange sounds, so she takes a trip to the Music Shop

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: Pepi Nana is big and Little Nana is small and when the two friends go for a walk together, they discover how they see things differently - and how much fun that can be

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Revival of the classic animation about the adventures of a clan of friendly aliens living on a small blue planet. Michael Palin narrates

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Tombliboos ride in the Pinky Ponk, and have fun guessing who will be the next person to climb on board

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Jennie McAlpine reads I Don't Want Curly Hair!, by Laura Ellen Anderson

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate, and reports from around Wales, presented by James Williams

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and the Rev Kate Bottley present topical debate covering religious, moral and ethical issues, with guest panellists and contributions from viewers

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts, as experts Philip Serrell and Richard Madley help teams scouring the streets of Hungerford, Berkshire, pick up items to sell for profit at auction

programme.title: Iolo's Pembrokeshire
programme.desc: Iolo Williams explores Pembrokeshire's stunning landscape and goes in search of its hidden wildlife spots, finding a rare butterfly and an old oil storage site where hundreds of orchids have bloomed. Iolo also walks round St David's Head, where he finds fearsome looking beetles and Britain's only venomous snake

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Novak Djokovic v Matteo Berrettini. Sue Barker presents live coverage on day 13 of the tournament at the All England Club, where the men's singles final takes centre stage. Defending champion Djokovic is chasing his sixth title here, while Berrettini is playing in his first Grand Slam final

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Italy v England (Kick-off 8.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the final, as the champions are crowned at Wembley Stadium. After a nervy semi-final appearance that saw them overcome a deficit for the first time during the tournament, Gareth Southgate's England players secured their place in the final of a major competition for just the second time in history. Their opponents have a rich pedigree, having won the World Cup four times and the European Championships once, and come into this match on the back of a 32-game unbeaten run. With analysis from Alan Shearer, Rio Ferdinand and Jurgen Klinsmann

programme.title: Crouchy's Year Late Euros: Live
programme.desc: Former England striker Peter Crouch gives his unique take on the latest Euro 2020 action alongside Maya Jama, with music from Alex Horne and the Horne Section

programme.title: Krept and Konan: We Are England
programme.desc: Documentary following the rap duo as they set out to make the England anthem for the Euro 2020 tournament. Along the way they get to know the squad, meeting players including Everton's Dominic Calvert-Lewin, Aston Villa's Tyrone Mings and West Ham's Declan Rice, and ask manager Gareth Southgate which ingredients he would like in the final song. They also seek advice from fellow artists Aitch and Big Zuu

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Italy v England. Another chance to see the showpiece match, as the tournament concluded at Wembley Stadium

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass look back at their investigation into the case of two men sentenced to death for the murder of a Liverpool woman in 1951

programme.title: Island Medics

programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: WPC 56
programme.desc: Drama centred on the fortunes of police officer Gina Dawson, who faces an uphill battle to carve out a successful career for herself in the male-dominated world of the 1950s. Brinford Police Station is rocked by a shocking discovery in an abandoned mine, while two senior officers investigate an attack on a young woman. Jennie Jacques and John Light star

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and brothers David and Harry Rich brothers reveal their favourite makeovers and also offer gardening tips and ideas. The first edition features a selection of family gardens, including a weird and wonderful Alice in Wonderland-inspired makeover, and one with a basketball court created for two children with mobility issues

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple who want to move from Surrey to Devon, hoping to find a sizeable home to fit three generations of their family and all for a budget of £800,000

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Master craftsman Simon Parfett and his team help people with a little land or a beautiful garden generate extra income by creating their own unique B&Bs. They begin by heading to the picturesque Wye Valley to help George and Al transform their battered old family camper van

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charlie Ross and Izzie Balmer start their hunt in Stow-on-the-Wold, Gloucestershire, buying items including a military first-aid kit and a toast rack to sell at auction in Cardiff

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Iolo's Pembrokeshire
programme.desc: Iolo searches the lower slopes of the Preseli Mountains and finds newly arrived migrant birds, including one which nests under rocks. He also hunts for the lights produced by glow worms near Martin's Haven, and watches an iconic nocturnal bird, the barn owl, hunt over rough grassland at dusk

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chelsea and Gray cause havoc at his work function when they make the most of the free drinks, embarrassing themselves in front of Gray's boss Laura. Honey reaches out to a grief-stricken Suki, Sheree gives Lola a word of warning and Jay offers Billy an olive branch

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: BBC correspondent Lucy Adams is one of more than a million people in the UK with Long Covid. In his programme, she reports on what the future holds for sufferers of long Covid symptoms, talking to patients, doctors with increasing caseloads, and scientists carrying out research into the condition

programme.title: Gold Rush: Our Race to Olympic Glory
programme.desc: Britain makes its bid to host the 2012 games, while the British team goes in search of new talent, including a nine-year-old Tom Daley beginning his sporting career. Christine Ohuruogu, the potential face of the 2012 games, discusses her dramatic fall from grace after missing three drugs tests, and her subsequent return to the track. The episode concludes with the 2008 games in Beijing, where Britain turned its fortunes around with a haul of 51 medals

programme.title: High: Confessions of an Ibiza Drug Mule
programme.desc: Michaella discusses how she claimed to have been forced into smuggling in the hope of getting a reduced sentence, only to find herself spending the first of what would be many days behind bars in the cells of the police's anti-narcotics division. However, her story soon caught the attention of the international press, with reporters around the world desperate for the inside scoop

programme.desc: The team recalls some of their favourite quirky garden rescues, including a music festival-inspired plot featuring an air-raid shelter, and a fusion of Yorkshire and Japan

programme.desc: Antiques experts Charlie Ross and Izzie Balmer head to Surrey, where Izzie finds a piece of jewellery that causes quite a stir at auction, and Charlie uncovers a rare table

programme.title: EastEnders

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Dom is asked to give a keynote speech at Jeni's fundraiser, but his elation is short-lived when mum Carole comes in with a burn on her arm. Is she hiding something? The cat is out of the bag when Kylie blurts out Ange's secret to Josh, while Max desperately tries to rally the troops after the disastrous findings of the Care Quality Commission

programme.title: Gravity (2013)
programme.desc: Two astronauts are left stranded in orbit above the Earth when the debris from an exploding satellite destroys their shuttle. Left drifting in space with no way to contact mission control, they begin a perilous journey to reach the safety of a space station before their oxygen supply runs out. Thriller, starring Sandra Bullock and George Clooney

programme.title: High: Confessions of an Ibiza Drug Mule
programme.desc: Michaella explains she was faced with a dilemma - stick to her story about being forced to carry the drugs and face 15 years in jail, or come clean and break her mother's heart

programme.desc: Norman tries to keep up appearances as he makes a troubling discovery, while Dylan and Emma welcome an unexpected guest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Patrick tries to get through to an increasingly agitated Isaac, asking him where Ruby and Martin's children are and promising that he will not let the police hurt him. Peter is upset at the turn of events with Ash so Kathy tells him a few home truths, Chelsea lands herself in a sticky situation at Gray's house and Tommy's skills are tested at the football trial

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Dom is asked to give a keynote speech at Jeni's fundraiser, but his elation is short-lived when mum Carole comes in with a burn on her arm. Is she hiding something? The cat is out of the bag when Kylie blurts out Ange's secret to Josh, while Max desperately tries to rally the troops after the disastrous findings of the Care Quality Commission

programme.title: Gravity (2013)
programme.desc: Two astronauts are left stranded in orbit above the Earth when the debris from an exploding satellite destroys their shuttle. Left drifting in space with no way to contact mission control, they begin a perilous journey to reach the safety of a space station before their oxygen supply runs out. Thriller, starring Sandra Bullock and George Clooney

programme.title: High: Confessions of an Ibiza Drug Mule
programme.desc: Michaella explains she was faced with a dilemma - stick to her story about being forced to carry the drugs and face 15 years in jail, or come clean and break her mother's heart

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman tries to keep up appearances as he makes a troubling discovery, while Dylan and Emma welcome an unexpected guest

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and the Rev Kate Bottley present topical debate covering religious, moral and ethical issues, with guest panellists and contributions from viewers

programme.title: Sunday Morning Stories
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley shares her favourite tales of faith, hope and spirituality, beginning by speaking to people who have found the faith and courage to change their lives, including a young man who has turned his life around after a stint in prison

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts, as experts Philip Serrell and Richard Madley help teams scouring the streets of Hungerford, Berkshire, pick up items to sell for profit at auction

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Novak Djokovic v Matteo Berrettini. Sue Barker presents live coverage on day 13 of the tournament at the All England Club, where the men's singles final takes centre stage. Defending champion Djokovic is chasing his sixth title here, while Berrettini is playing in his first Grand Slam final

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Italy v England (Kick-off 8.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the final, as the champions are crowned at Wembley Stadium. After a nervy semi-final appearance that saw them overcome a deficit for the first time during the tournament, Gareth Southgate's England players secured their place in the final of a major competition for just the second time in history. Their opponents have a rich pedigree, having won the World Cup four times and the European Championships once, and come into this match on the back of a 32-game unbeaten run. With analysis from Alan Shearer, Rio Ferdinand and Jurgen Klinsmann

programme.title: Crouchy's Year Late Euros: Live
programme.desc: Former England striker Peter Crouch gives his unique take on the latest Euro 2020 action alongside Maya Jama, with music from Alex Horne and the Horne Section

programme.title: Krept and Konan: We Are England
programme.desc: Documentary following the rap duo as they set out to make the England anthem for the Euro 2020 tournament. Along the way they get to know the squad, meeting players including Everton's Dominic Calvert-Lewin, Aston Villa's Tyrone Mings and West Ham's Declan Rice, and ask manager Gareth Southgate which ingredients he would like in the final song. They also seek advice from fellow artists Aitch and Big Zuu

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Italy v England. Another chance to see the showpiece match, as the tournament concluded at Wembley Stadium

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit two neighbouring cottages in Derbyshire, a terrace in Bolton and a semi in Leeds before they are sold under the hammer

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass look back at their investigation into the case of two men sentenced to death for the murder of a Liverpool woman in 1951

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Further action from the UK's most remote hospital. A carrot cake causes an emergency for a nut allergy sufferer who's airlifted to Shetland

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents the show from Wrexham, and discovers the story of The Ladies of Llangollen. Roo Irvine and Philip Serrell provide expert advice to today's teams

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: WPC 56
programme.desc: Drama centred on the fortunes of police officer Gina Dawson, who faces an uphill battle to carve out a successful career for herself in the male-dominated world of the 1950s. Brinford Police Station is rocked by a shocking discovery in an abandoned mine, while two senior officers investigate an attack on a young woman. Jennie Jacques and John Light star

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and brothers David and Harry Rich brothers reveal their favourite makeovers and also offer gardening tips and ideas. The first edition features a selection of family gardens, including a weird and wonderful Alice in Wonderland-inspired makeover, and one with a basketball court created for two children with mobility issues

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple who want to move from Surrey to Devon, hoping to find a sizeable home to fit three generations of their family and all for a budget of £800,000

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Master craftsman Simon Parfett and his team help people with a little land or a beautiful garden generate extra income by creating their own unique B&Bs. They begin by heading to the picturesque Wye Valley to help George and Al transform their battered old family camper van

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charlie Ross and Izzie Balmer start their hunt in Stow-on-the-Wold, Gloucestershire, buying items including a military first-aid kit and a toast rack to sell at auction in Cardiff

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Life Is Magic
programme.desc: Illusionist Joel M meets car-crash survivors Luke and his mother to plan a magical party to celebrate the end of the dreaded transfer test

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chelsea and Gray cause havoc at his work function when they make the most of the free drinks, embarrassing themselves in front of Gray's boss Laura. Honey reaches out to a grief-stricken Suki, Sheree gives Lola a word of warning and Jay offers Billy an olive branch

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: BBC correspondent Lucy Adams is one of more than a million people in the UK with Long Covid. In his programme, she reports on what the future holds for sufferers of long Covid symptoms, talking to patients, doctors with increasing caseloads, and scientists carrying out research into the condition

programme.title: Gold Rush: Our Race to Olympic Glory
programme.desc: Britain makes its bid to host the 2012 games, while the British team goes in search of new talent, including a nine-year-old Tom Daley beginning his sporting career. Christine Ohuruogu, the potential face of the 2012 games, discusses her dramatic fall from grace after missing three drugs tests, and her subsequent return to the track. The episode concludes with the 2008 games in Beijing, where Britain turned its fortunes around with a haul of 51 medals

programme.title: The Twelfth
programme.desc: Helen Mark and the commentary team take a look back at some of the sights and the sounds from today's parades from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: A couple from Manchester answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Our World
programme.desc: A storm is brewing in the South China Sea. China claims large swathes of it as its own, but President Biden has recently warned that it must be open for trade and navigation. Caught in the middle are Filipino fishermen, who increasingly find themselves shadowed by the Chinese coastguard and militia boats as they try to earn a living. Howard Johnson sails to Scarborough Shoal, a large coral reef 120 nautical miles off the coast of the Philippines, to discover how far China is prepared to go to assert its power and influence in the region.

There's just one tiny snag - a ferocious and foul Beast is guarding the treasure. One wrong move and it could be unleashed!

programme.title: The Void
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Ashley Banjo and Fleur East in which contestants take on a wide range of demanding mental and physical challenges to win a jackpot of £25,000 - all while navigating the terrifying prospect of falling into a huge tank holding 520,000 litres of water called the Void. Among those taking part in the first edition are a weightlifting champion, a former US paratrooper and a professional jester

programme.title: Australian Ninja Warrior
programme.desc: Rebecca Maddern, Ben Fordham and Freddie Flintoff present the second part of the Grand Final, with the remaining contestants stepping up to stages two and three. The one who makes it furthest will pocket A$100,000, but the prize for scaling Mt Midoriyama is a cool A$300,000

programme.title: Minions (2015)
programme.desc: A race of strange, babbling yellow creatures has served the cause of evil throughout the history of the world. In the 1960s, three of these eternal henchmen get a job working for a criminal genius who is hatching a plot against the British crown - but while carrying out her orders, they uncover a threat to their whole species. Animated comedy, with the voices of Sandra Bullock, Pierre Coffin, Jon Hamm and Michael Keaton

programme.title: The Italian Job (1969)
programme.desc: A crook assembles a motley gang to steal £4million in gold bullion from an Italian armoured car, which brings Turin to a standstill and enables the robbers to make a quick getaway in a fleet of Minis - but they end up falling foul of the Mafia along the way. Crime comedy, starring Michael Caine, Noel Coward, Benny Hill, Tony Beckley and John Le Mesurier

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: Italy v England (Kick-off 8.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the showpiece match, as the champions are crowned at Wembley Stadium. Having overcome Denmark 2-1 after extra time in the semi-finals, Gareth Southgate's men will hope to follow in the footsteps of England legends such as Bobby Moore, Geoff Hurst and Nobby Stiles by winning a major tournament at Wembley Stadium. To do so they must overcome a nation with a rich history of winning trophies, who, like England, progressed to this showpiece match with an unbeaten record, though they were made to work hard for their penalty shootout victory over Spain in the last four. The sides last met on this ground in 2018, sharing a 1-1 draw in a friendly, but both teams are much-changed since then. With analysis from Roy Keane, Gary Neville and Ian Wright, pitchside insight by Seema Jaswal, Ashley Cole and Emma Hayes, and commentary from Sam Matterface and Lee Dixon

programme.title: England's Grand
programme.desc: Gareth Southgate, Harry Kane and Ian Wright join a host of players, authors and experts for an A-Z journey through the history of the England football team. The documentary brings together previously unheard stories, anecdotes and unearthed archive in a celebration of the 147 years leading up to England's 1,000th match, which took place on Thursday 14th November 2019 against Montenegro

programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

Meanwhile at the factory, Beth and Sean urge Fiz to sign up to a dating website, and Steve reveals that he's raised £96,000 for Oliver's fund, but feels it's time to take a step back. Curtis tells Emma about his plan to organise a charity naked calendar to help Steve reach his target of £100k

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by friend and Michelin-starred chef Mark Jordan, riding around Jersey on mini motorbikes while exploring the island's culinary delights. James samples one of Jersey's most famous exports - the Jersey Royal potato - which he uses to cook up a squid risotto with a difference

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alya tells Ryan they are finished, and confronts Daisy in the cafe. Mr Hendricks returns the urn to the undertakers', and Todd checks it - only to find the cash inside has gone. Fiz meets up with her date in the bistro, only to discover that Chris is a reporter hoping to run a story on aggrieved women who've been dumped for a younger model

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell present the stories of two people trying to unravel family mysteries, with both looking for a birth parent they have never met. Fifty-seven-year-old quarry worker John Hacking was told that his birth mother left him as a baby in a pram outside a block of flats in Buxton and has been searching for her since he was 17 with no luck, while Debra Spark has never met her Italian father Ernesto, who got together with Debra's mother Olive in Milan in the 1960s

programme.desc: Game show hosted by Ashley Banjo and Fleur East in which contestants take on a wide range of demanding mental and physical challenges to win a jackpot of £25,000 - all while navigating the terrifying prospect of falling into a huge tank holding 520,000 litres of water called the Void. Among those taking part in the first edition are a weightlifting champion, a former US paratrooper and a professional jester

programme.title: Australian Ninja Warrior
programme.desc: Rebecca Maddern, Ben Fordham and Freddie Flintoff present the second part of the Grand Final, with the remaining contestants stepping up to stages two and three. The one who makes it furthest will pocket A$100,000, but the prize for scaling Mt Midoriyama is a cool A$300,000

programme.title: Minions (2015)
programme.desc: A race of strange, babbling yellow creatures has served the cause of evil throughout the history of the world. In the 1960s, three of these eternal henchmen get a job working for a criminal genius who is hatching a plot against the British crown - but while carrying out her orders, they uncover a threat to their whole species. Animated comedy, with the voices of Sandra Bullock, Pierre Coffin, Jon Hamm and Michael Keaton

programme.title: The Italian Job (1969)
programme.desc: A crook assembles a motley gang to steal £4million in gold bullion from an Italian armoured car, which brings Turin to a standstill and enables the robbers to make a quick getaway in a fleet of Minis - but they end up falling foul of the Mafia along the way. Crime comedy, starring Michael Caine, Noel Coward, Benny Hill, Tony Beckley and John Le Mesurier

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: Italy v England (Kick-off 8.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the showpiece match, as the champions are crowned at Wembley Stadium. Having overcome Denmark 2-1 after extra time in the semi-finals, Gareth Southgate's men will hope to follow in the footsteps of England legends such as Bobby Moore, Geoff Hurst and Nobby Stiles by winning a major tournament at Wembley Stadium. To do so they must overcome a nation with a rich history of winning trophies, who, like England, progressed to this showpiece match with an unbeaten record, though they were made to work hard for their penalty shootout victory over Spain in the last four. The sides last met on this ground in 2018, sharing a 1-1 draw in a friendly, but both teams are much-changed since then. With analysis from Roy Keane, Gary Neville and Ian Wright, pitchside insight by Seema Jaswal, Ashley Cole and Emma Hayes, and commentary from Sam Matterface and Lee Dixon

programme.title: England's Grand
programme.desc: Gareth Southgate, Harry Kane and Ian Wright join a host of players, authors and experts for an A-Z journey through the history of the England football team. The documentary brings together previously unheard stories, anecdotes and unearthed archive in a celebration of the 147 years leading up to England's 1,000th match, which took place on Thursday 14th November 2019 against Montenegro

Fifty-seven-year-old quarry worker John Hacking was told that his birth mother left him as a baby in a pram outside a block of flats in Buxton and has been searching for her since he was 17 with no luck, while Debra Spark has never met her Italian father Ernesto, who got together with Debra's mother Olive in Milan in the 1960s

programme.title: Stardust (2007)
programme.desc: A young shop assistant in Victorian England enters a magical parallel world in search of a fallen star to give to the girl of his dreams. Fantasy adventure, based on Neil Gaiman's novel, with Charlie Cox, Claire Danes, Michelle Pfeiffer, Robert De Niro, Mark Strong and Sienna Miller. Narrated by Ian McKellen

programme.desc: Three single men in Cardiff try to win the affection of Ben Brown - also known as Mr Gay Wales 2017 - by each buying their potential date an outfit they think he might like

programme.title: The Cabins
programme.desc: Dating show in which single people are paired up and sent to three remote log cabins and have to decide after 24 hours whether to extend their stay or leave

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: The village grieves, Gabby and Noah bond, and David has a re-think

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: In the Rovers, Ryan begs Daisy not to let slip that he was with her last night. Meanwhile at the factory, Beth and Sean urge Fiz to sign up to a dating website, and Steve reveals that he's raised £96,000 for Oliver's fund, but feels it's time to take a step back. Curtis tells Emma about his plan to organise a charity naked calendar to help Steve reach his target of £100k

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alya tells Ryan they are finished, and confronts Daisy in the cafe. Mr Hendricks returns the urn to the undertakers', and Todd checks it - only to find the cash inside has gone. Fiz meets up with her date in the bistro, only to discover that Chris is a reporter hoping to run a story on aggrieved women who've been dumped for a younger model

programme.title: The Ellen DeGeneres Show
programme.desc: Kristen Bell discusses Happy Dance and Hello Bello with guest host Chelsea Handler, while Wiz Khalifa talks about Duncanville

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: The show returns to Cardiff, where beautician Leah is waiting to be swept off her feet by the style choices of either law graduate Lloyd, judo champion Aron or student Daffyd. With only their outfits to help her decide, which lad will Leah choose to join her for a romantic date?

programme.title: The Cabins
programme.desc: One of the group's motives come under question, romance blossoms elsewhere, and insecurities could rear their head and bring someone's luxurious stay to a premature end

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill unveils more digital disasters. Featuring a wallet and key stealing monkey and a semi-skilled scientist, while helmets are definitely required for a collection of bicycle accidents

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, including a drone flying into a child's face, a unicycling Scotsman and a builder being crushed by a brick wall. Plus, why all members of the Conservative Party have conjunctivitis

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity special of the game show, asking contestants Ellie Simmonds, Fay Ripley and Jimmy Carr to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. After the first elimination, the two remaining players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Amy turns a boring Christmas Eve at Cloud 9 into a holiday adventure. Meanwhile, Jonah and Garrett's roommate issues boil over

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Dina and Cheyenne snoop through the staff members' social media feeds after a controversial video is posted. Glenn suspects Mateo is guilty of fraud

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: Gene disapproves of a new playground game that is throwing the school into chaos, while Linda develops a storyteller persona when she fills in at the local library

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: The kids set out to find a secret room filled with homemade ceramics which, according to urban legend, has been boarded up for years at their school

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: The relationship-based reality show continues as the singles get on with their daily routine of dating, flirting and dumping. Presented by Laura Whitmore

programme.title: Shopping with Keith Lemon
programme.desc: Keith takes TV presenter Maya Jama for a round of crazy golf and has a playdate with Take That's Howard Donald at Diggerland

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After seeing a news report on rising crime in Quahog, the Griffins return home to find they have been robbed. Peter announces they are selling their house and moving to a farm in the countryside, but when they begin to struggle financially, he starts an illicit drug business

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois is forced to find a job when the family's domestic finances are stretched. She is 'discovered' by a voiceover talent agent who runs an adult-only phone line and pays his employees generously for services rendered. As she settles into her new role, she gets a surprise call. Meanwhile, Peter acquires a pet falcon

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: In his continuing quest to meet girls, Steve decides to try to earn a letter jacket by joining the school's water polo team. When his initial attempt fails, he enlists Klaus' help to make the squad, and soon becomes the team's star player. Meanwhile, Stan and Roger buy a sailboat, but find life on water is not so easy as they imagined

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Francine is up for an award for her lonely housewife novel, so the family heads to the Big Apple for the ceremony. Meanwhile, Stan and Hayley hang out with actor Robert Wuhl, and Steve and Roger recreate their 'Wheels and the Legman' persona

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: Gene disapproves of a new playground game that is throwing the school into chaos, while Linda develops a storyteller persona when she fills in at the local library

programme.title: Totally Bonkers Guinness World Records
programme.desc: Matt Edmondson narrates a selection of some of the most incredible and peculiar record-breaking attempts

programme.title: Totally Bonkers Guinness World Records
programme.desc: Matt Edmondson narrates a selection of unusual records, including the world's smallest horse, the world's fastest human crab, and the highest note ever sung

programme.desc: Jimmy struggles with admitting the truth. Meena is fuming, and Tracy pours out her feelings to Nate

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: A misunderstanding between Sid and Betty leads to chaos and confusion. Family comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: A death in the family causes problems for Sid. Comedy, starring Diana Coupland

programme.title: Bless This House (1972)
programme.desc: Middle-aged father Sid Abbott's dream of building an illegal whisky still in the shed is continually frustrated by the antics of his son and daughter. However, he has a bigger problem in store when new neighbours move in next door and conflict instantly erupts. Comedy, based on the sitcom, starring Sid James, Diana Coupland and Sally Geeson

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An American tourist's body is discovered in her hotel room, apparently dead from a heart attack - but a valuable Anglo-Saxon jewel she intended to donate to the Ashmolean Museum is missing and several interested parties are acting strangely. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Simon Callow, Roberta Taylor and Kenneth Cranham

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The father of a missing schoolgirl demands action, angry that a six-month investigation has failed to uncover any leads. Morse ends up being assigned the case - and discovers some unpleasant truths about her family and teachers during the course of his inquiries. John Thaw stars as the Oxford detective, with Kevin Whately, Peter Woodthorpe, Peter McEnery, Elizabeth Hurley and Frances Tomelty

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When wealthy landowner Gregory Lancaster's body goes missing on the night of his death, a sinister web of secrets and lies is exposed in the village of Little Malton. As DCI Barnaby, DS Nelson and new forensic pathologist Kam Karimore investigate the mysterious crime, they find themselves drawn into a macabre world of body-snatching during their quest to identify the perpetrator. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk, Helen Baxendale and Navin Chowdhry

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Forest ranger Felicity Ward is blinded by mysterious lights while driving at night in the village of Cooper Hill and the next morning her car is found abandoned at the roadside, followed closely by the discovery of her body. Visiting UFO spotters in the village are convinced aliens are responsible, but as Barnaby and Nelson try to get to the truth, they unearth suspicions, betrayals and long-buried secrets along the way. Michelle Collins, Steve Evets, Alison Steadman, Steve Oram and Pip Torrens guest star

programme.title: An Audience with Ronnie Corbett
programme.desc: The comedian entertains his peers, fans, friends and family with jokes and ramblings in a show recorded in 1997. Celebrity audience members include Donald Sinden, Martin Jarvis, Gorden Kaye, Lynda Bellingham, Norman Bowler, Kevin Lloyd and Desmond Llewelyn. Ronnie Barker also joins the host on stage to re-create some of the magic of the Two Ronnies

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis investigate the death of a maintenance engineer found shot in the head in the basement of the Bodleian Library. A search of the man's house reveals a stash of valuable volumes and a connection to the local Gamblers Anonymous group, and the detectives go on to expose a scam involving two Oxford academics. Drama, guest starring Neil Pearson and Haydn Gwynne, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Todd, determined to prove Summer can trust him, returns home and proposes to Billy. Izzy's work samples prove problematic for Carla. Fiz tells Tyrone that he needs to tell the girls about the baby today, and if he doesn't, she will

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Paul warns Billy that if he marries Todd, he will live to regret it. Carla tells Sarah that the situation with Izzy is untenable and if she refuses to come back to work, they'll have to get rid of her

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Todd heads home to find a hooded figure about to break into his flat. Daisy and Ryan plan their promo party in Victoria Garden for Simon's 18th birthday. Sarah tells Carla about Izzy's sudden resignation. Natasha relents and tells Nick he can see Sam

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Paul confronts Billy, demanding to know why he would let Summer hang out with Will when he nearly got her killed. Daisy makes a move on Ryan, Peter returns home from hospital, and George sings Sean's praises to a bemused Eileen

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Janice worries about Toyah going out with yobbish Dobber. Anne admits to Spider that she set Curly up. Jim takes Liz out for dinner, but it does not go as well as he hoped

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Toyah threatens to run away again if her parents stop her from seeing Dobber. Liz is upset that Jim is still his old aggressive self. Maxine fears Audrey intends to sack her

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Gavin tries to talk to Bernice, but she is not interested. Chris tells Zoe about a new business idea. News of Gavin's sexuality spreads around the pub

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Diane warns Ashley to keep his distance from Bernice. Viv gives Terry a roasting for working for the Tates. Bernice turns down Alan's offer to buy the pub

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Greengrass starts a devious plot involving the postal service, much to Blaketon's annoyance, and several people are suspected of attacking a TV repair man. Rural drama, starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean and Sid are faced with a dilemma when they notice strange goings-on next door. Vintage comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: The flatmates crave a bit of summer warmth, and Mrs Roper's sunlamp mysteriously disappears. Comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The badly beaten body of 18-year-old clerk Sylvia Kane is found in a pub car park by her boyfriend John Sanders. Called in to investigate, Morse finds a coded letter in her bag and sets about trying to decipher it, gradually unravelling a tangled web of hidden passion and corruption at the insurance office where she worked. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Anthony Bate, Terrence Hardiman, Jill Baker, Holly Aird and Ingrid Lacey

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mike has problems when a man held in custody drops dead. Bernie and Greengrass establish a taxi firm and look for ways to make a profit

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frankie confronts Zoe about her trip to Germany. Jack sells Sarah's car without her knowledge. Diane plans to sell her bed and breakfast to help fund Bernice's takeover at the pub

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Relations are still frosty at the Sugdens. Zoe and Ashley commiserate about how unlucky in love they are. Sarah pours her heart out about her marriage to Kathy

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alma tries to retrieve the security tape before it is wiped. Maxine works hard to prove her worth to Audrey. Liz is surprised when Michael kisses her

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Spider and the security guard find Anne's frozen body. Michael apologises to Liz for taking advantage of her. Jim signs up for wheelchair basketball

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Called to investigate the attempted theft of a headless skeleton from an archaeological dig, Wycliffe finds himself forced to deal with landowners whose archaic values are jarringly at odds with the modern world. Detective drama, starring Jack Shepherd

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: An international cycling competition comes to the Midsomer village of Burwood Mantle, but when the race leader is murdered, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson must investigate. During the race's finale, the victim overtook his team-mate to win, leaving the team owner annoyed that he disobeyed his orders, and the rival manager disappointed that his team lost. However, as the pair look into the case, they find themselves drawn into a competitive world of blackmail, bribery and bloodshed, where winning comes at any cost

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: An exciting offer for Edith divides opinion, Isobel throws Ethel a lifeline and Anna's perseverance finally pays off. Daisy is unhappy when Alfred takes a keen interest in newly arrived maid Ivy, while Matthew's concerns about the estate grow - but are his protests falling on deaf ears? Costume drama, starring Laura Carmichael, Penelope Wilton, Amy Nuttall, Joanne Froggatt and Dan Stevens

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The death of Oxford don RG Cole leads to the shady underworld of unlicensed boxing and the involvement of academic Richard Helm. Investigations reveal the dead man's student Jack Roth was involved in a complex love triangle, and when the young man's rival is found dead in an apparent suicide, Lewis suspects foul play and discovers an old connection to Helm - along with a link to his former mentor Morse. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. Frustrated that a number of cases have been knocked back by the Crown Prosecution Service, Banks and the team are called on to investigate the murder of teenager Ellie Clayton, daughter of a high-profile internet entrepreneur. The victim's background, together with the suggestion she may have been raped, means the incident is soon the subject of much media speculation, and strong evidence implicates a theatre director who ran a drama workshop the girl attended on the night she died. The detective believes he has found his man, but Morton fears her colleague isn't seeing the bigger picture

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. The collapse of Owen Pierce's trial has left the case wide open and there's still a killer on the loose, but Banks remains determined to build a watertight legal case against his prime suspect. As the investigation continues, Morton and Jackman uncover promising new leads focusing on Ellie's ex-boyfriend Tyler, but just as they seem to be getting close to the truth, another schoolgirl is murdered, putting Pierce in the spotlight once again

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An aristocrat goes missing, along with his collection of explicit erotic paintings - and Morse's investigation provides him with a close-up view of the upper crust's dark side. What is more, there is a further culture shock lying in wait - the prospect of working with a new female pathologist. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Amanda Hillwood, Patricia Hodge and Clifford Rose

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid's cousin visits the Abbott household, and the bemused family have a terrible time getting rid of him. Sid James stars

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Jo are entranced when they meet Robin's older brother. Comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An aristocrat goes missing, along with his collection of explicit erotic paintings - and Morse's investigation provides him with a close-up view of the upper crust's dark side. What is more, there is a further culture shock lying in wait - the prospect of working with a new female pathologist. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Amanda Hillwood, Patricia Hodge and Clifford Rose

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Eternal bachelor Greengrass falls in love with an aristocratic lady poacher, and an old flame of Mike's turns up out of the blue to complicate his life. Rural capers, starring Nick Berry, Natasha Dahlberg and Frances de la Tour

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris tells Zoe he has decided to go back to work. Emily is frantic with worry about her missing father. Jack promises a worried Andy that he will sort things out with Sarah

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kelly wants someone to talk to, but Chris tells her she should leave Roy. Turner convinces Diane to offer to help Bernice buy the Woolpack

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Natalie celebrates her birthday and engagement. Liz is torn as she is attracted to Michael but feels she cannot cheat on Jim. Alma is promoted to temporary manager at Freshco

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alma decides to take the job of manager until Curly returns. Des advises Kevin to leave Natalie alone and move on in his life. Sally turns to Gail for sympathy

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A comatose surfer is washed ashore in what seems to have been a freak accident, until sinister facts emerge and Wycliffe is called in. It is not long before the team uncovers a conspiracy - but the case is hindered by a leak to the Press that threatens not only the investigation but also DI Lane's career. Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Art comes to the Midsomer village of Angel's Rise with the opening of a new sculpture park, but when its launch is marred by murder, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson are called to investigate. However, they must deal with an apparently unexplainable broken locket found near the scene of the crime, a host of suspects, and a complex web of deception. As the crime solving duo inspect further, they find they have to get creative to solve a case where art imitates death and everything has a deeper meaning

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Tempers fray as a kind gesture by Isobel backfires, while Mary fights Branson's corner when his decisions are put under scrutiny. Robert and Cora's relationship is under pressure, but can Violet make them see things differently? Bates goes to extreme lengths as his chance of freedom slips away. Costume drama, starring Penelope Wilton, Hugh Bonneville and Elizabeth McGovern

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Schoolteacher Connie Bishop keeps waking in the night, convinced she has an intruder, but most of the villagers in Badger's Drift dismiss her fears as an overactive imagination. Days later, a local councillor thought to have been obsessed with her is found murdered in her garden, and the ensuing investigation by Barnaby and Jones uncovers jealous rivals, ruthless social climbers and a cottage with a few secrets. Guest starring Paul Kaye, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: The crime drama starring Stephen Tompkinson returns with the first of a two-part story. A man and woman claiming to be social workers visit a mother and tell her they need to take her son away. However, when they fail to bring him back, it transpires that the pair are not known by the authorities. Banks is drawn into a full-scale search for the missing boy, but new evidence and inconsistent witness statements cause the detective to question the truth of what he has been told. Co-starring Andrea Lowe, Caroline Catz, Christine Bottomley and John Lightbody

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. The detective now has a murder inquiry on his hands as the body found on the moors is identified as 11-year-old Andre Petri. With Kyle Heath still missing, Banks divides his team to search for a connection between the cases, and when post-mortem examinations show Andre was killed by a heroin overdose, it appears the two boys are linked by the illegal drug trade. Starring Stephen Tompkinson, with Andrea Lowe, Caroline Catz, Christine Bottomley and John Lightbody

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The master of a prestigious college asks Morse to investigate the disappearance of one of his colleagues. The subsequent discovery of a dismembered torso soon has everyone fearing the worst - until the missing man turns up alive and well. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, James Grout, Beatie Edney, Sian Thomas and Barry Foster

programme.desc: Toyah threatens to run away again if her parents stop her from seeing Dobber. Liz is upset that Jim is still his old aggressive self. Maxine fears Audrey intends to sack her

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Gavin tries to talk to Bernice, but she is not interested. Chris tells Zoe about a new business idea. News of Gavin's sexuality spreads around the pub

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Diane warns Ashley to keep his distance from Bernice. Viv gives Terry a roasting for working for the Tates. Bernice turns down Alan's offer to buy the pub

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Greengrass starts a devious plot involving the postal service, much to Blaketon's annoyance, and several people are suspected of attacking a TV repair man. Rural drama, starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean and Sid are faced with a dilemma when they notice strange goings-on next door. Vintage comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: The flatmates crave a bit of summer warmth, and Mrs Roper's sunlamp mysteriously disappears. Comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The badly beaten body of 18-year-old clerk Sylvia Kane is found in a pub car park by her boyfriend John Sanders. Called in to investigate, Morse finds a coded letter in her bag and sets about trying to decipher it, gradually unravelling a tangled web of hidden passion and corruption at the insurance office where she worked. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Anthony Bate, Terrence Hardiman, Jill Baker, Holly Aird and Ingrid Lacey

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mike has problems when a man held in custody drops dead. Bernie and Greengrass establish a taxi firm and look for ways to make a profit

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frankie confronts Zoe about her trip to Germany. Jack sells Sarah's car without her knowledge. Diane plans to sell her bed and breakfast to help fund Bernice's takeover at the pub

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Relations are still frosty at the Sugdens. Zoe and Ashley commiserate about how unlucky in love they are. Sarah pours her heart out about her marriage to Kathy

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alma tries to retrieve the security tape before it is wiped. Maxine works hard to prove her worth to Audrey. Liz is surprised when Michael kisses her

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Spider and the security guard find Anne's frozen body. Michael apologises to Liz for taking advantage of her. Jim signs up for wheelchair basketball

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Called to investigate the attempted theft of a headless skeleton from an archaeological dig, Wycliffe finds himself forced to deal with landowners whose archaic values are jarringly at odds with the modern world. Detective drama, starring Jack Shepherd

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: An international cycling competition comes to the Midsomer village of Burwood Mantle, but when the race leader is murdered, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson must investigate. During the race's finale, the victim overtook his team-mate to win, leaving the team owner annoyed that he disobeyed his orders, and the rival manager disappointed that his team lost. However, as the pair look into the case, they find themselves drawn into a competitive world of blackmail, bribery and bloodshed, where winning comes at any cost

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: An exciting offer for Edith divides opinion, Isobel throws Ethel a lifeline and Anna's perseverance finally pays off. Daisy is unhappy when Alfred takes a keen interest in newly arrived maid Ivy, while Matthew's concerns about the estate grow - but are his protests falling on deaf ears? Costume drama, starring Laura Carmichael, Penelope Wilton, Amy Nuttall, Joanne Froggatt and Dan Stevens

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The death of Oxford don RG Cole leads to the shady underworld of unlicensed boxing and the involvement of academic Richard Helm. Investigations reveal the dead man's student Jack Roth was involved in a complex love triangle, and when the young man's rival is found dead in an apparent suicide, Lewis suspects foul play and discovers an old connection to Helm - along with a link to his former mentor Morse. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. Frustrated that a number of cases have been knocked back by the Crown Prosecution Service, Banks and the team are called on to investigate the murder of teenager Ellie Clayton, daughter of a high-profile internet entrepreneur. The victim's background, together with the suggestion she may have been raped, means the incident is soon the subject of much media speculation, and strong evidence implicates a theatre director who ran a drama workshop the girl attended on the night she died. The detective believes he has found his man, but Morton fears her colleague isn't seeing the bigger picture

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. The collapse of Owen Pierce's trial has left the case wide open and there's still a killer on the loose, but Banks remains determined to build a watertight legal case against his prime suspect. As the investigation continues, Morton and Jackman uncover promising new leads focusing on Ellie's ex-boyfriend Tyler, but just as they seem to be getting close to the truth, another schoolgirl is murdered, putting Pierce in the spotlight once again

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An aristocrat goes missing, along with his collection of explicit erotic paintings - and Morse's investigation provides him with a close-up view of the upper crust's dark side. What is more, there is a further culture shock lying in wait - the prospect of working with a new female pathologist. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Amanda Hillwood, Patricia Hodge and Clifford Rose

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kelly wants someone to talk to, but Chris tells her she should leave Roy. Turner convinces Diane to offer to help Bernice buy the Woolpack

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alma decides to take the job of manager until Curly returns. Des advises Kevin to leave Natalie alone and move on in his life. Sally turns to Gail for sympathy

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A comatose surfer is washed ashore in what seems to have been a freak accident, until sinister facts emerge and Wycliffe is called in. It is not long before the team uncovers a conspiracy - but the case is hindered by a leak to the Press that threatens not only the investigation but also DI Lane's career. Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Art comes to the Midsomer village of Angel's Rise with the opening of a new sculpture park, but when its launch is marred by murder, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson are called to investigate. However, they must deal with an apparently unexplainable broken locket found near the scene of the crime, a host of suspects, and a complex web of deception. As the crime solving duo inspect further, they find they have to get creative to solve a case where art imitates death and everything has a deeper meaning

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Tempers fray as a kind gesture by Isobel backfires, while Mary fights Branson's corner when his decisions are put under scrutiny. Robert and Cora's relationship is under pressure, but can Violet make them see things differently? Bates goes to extreme lengths as his chance of freedom slips away. Costume drama, starring Penelope Wilton, Hugh Bonneville and Elizabeth McGovern

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Schoolteacher Connie Bishop keeps waking in the night, convinced she has an intruder, but most of the villagers in Badger's Drift dismiss her fears as an overactive imagination. Days later, a local councillor thought to have been obsessed with her is found murdered in her garden, and the ensuing investigation by Barnaby and Jones uncovers jealous rivals, ruthless social climbers and a cottage with a few secrets. Guest starring Paul Kaye, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: The crime drama starring Stephen Tompkinson returns with the first of a two-part story. A man and woman claiming to be social workers visit a mother and tell her they need to take her son away. However, when they fail to bring him back, it transpires that the pair are not known by the authorities. Banks is drawn into a full-scale search for the missing boy, but new evidence and inconsistent witness statements cause the detective to question the truth of what he has been told. Co-starring Andrea Lowe, Caroline Catz, Christine Bottomley and John Lightbody

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. The detective now has a murder inquiry on his hands as the body found on the moors is identified as 11-year-old Andre Petri. With Kyle Heath still missing, Banks divides his team to search for a connection between the cases, and when post-mortem examinations show Andre was killed by a heroin overdose, it appears the two boys are linked by the illegal drug trade. Starring Stephen Tompkinson, with Andrea Lowe, Caroline Catz, Christine Bottomley and John Lightbody

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The master of a prestigious college asks Morse to investigate the disappearance of one of his colleagues. The subsequent discovery of a dismembered torso soon has everyone fearing the worst - until the missing man turns up alive and well. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, James Grout, Beatie Edney, Sian Thomas and Barry Foster

Extreme angler Jeremy Wade travels to the tropical paradise, with his quest for answers taking him to dangerous new depths and closer than ever to a real killer

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: Stuart Little (1999)
programme.desc: An American couple decide their son needs a brother, and plan to adopt another child - but when they get to the orphanage they are greeted by a charming talking mouse named Stuart, who fits the bill perfectly. However, their jealous cat hatches a plot to get rid of the new arrival. Comedy adventure, starring Geena Davis and Hugh Laurie, with the voice of Michael J Fox

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Black rhino Kitani is due to give birth at Chester Zoo and help swell the ranks of this endangered species, but, having lost three previous calves, it is a worrying time and the keepers are concerned that things aren't going to plan. The zoo also has four rare new arrivals called tenrecs, mysterious mammals from Madagascar and, with so little known about them, the keepers try everything they can to help them breed, while red river hog Mali has given birth to twins, but tearaway dad Confetti is a threat to his own babies

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Natasha Cargill wants to build a home shaped like two enormous periscopes in rural Norfolk, but to obtain planning permission, she has to ensure not only that the materials are sustainable, but also agree to measure the transportation used to deliver them. If these strict criteria are not met, she won't be allowed to live there, and to add to the pressure, she has just £330,000 to spend on construction and six months to complete the project. Kevin McCloud follows her progress

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: It's springtime at the chateau, and with the wedding season just a few weeks away, completing phase two of the basement project is becoming an urgent priority for Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree. To cater for all their summer guests, a much-needed utility and laundry room is about to be plumbed in. Angel comes up with a unique way of preserving the 150-year-old wallpaper, while Dick wrestles with a rake while working on the meadow

programme.title: Scotland's Coastal Railways with Julie Walters
programme.desc: The actress travels the beautiful coastal railways of Scotland, and meets the vibrant characters who live and work along its shoreline. In North Berwick she chats to Jane McMinn, IT consultant-turned-master mariner and lobster breeder. Other destinations include Edinburgh, where Julie discovers the intriguing connections between the railway and the famous Balmoral Hotel's Palm Court and Clock Tower

programme.desc: Anne Robinson and Rachel Riley host the game show where contestants pit their wits against each the clock. Philippa Perry is in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.desc: The final episode of the series pays a visit to the Isles of Scilly, meeting the owners of a vineyard who are planning to retire as they show their successors the ropes. On the mainland in Cornwall, there's an ambitious project underway to build the world's largest labyrinth. Plus, the danger posed by abandoned fishing nets

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A rugby player is scanned for spinal damage following a collision during a match, and describes her struggles with mental health issues as she awaits the results. A teacher is knocked off his bicycle after receiving some bad news, while a man who broke two bones in his ankle after a fall is assisted by his dead wife's best friend

programme.title: Kathy Burke: Money Talks
programme.desc: Part two of two. Kathy explores the idea of escaping poverty through hard work, and considers the unseen obstacles that make this kind of social change difficult. She visits one of the most deprived towns in Britain and meets a local man trying to build community spirit, and discovers a community in Somerset for people who have rejected capitalism

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Sean Lock and Daisy May Cooper take on guest team captain Richard Ayoade and Ivo Graham. Adam Buxton is in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent, and Joe Wilkinson joins Rachel Riley at the numbers board

programme.title: Murder in the Outback: The Falconio and Lees Mystery
programme.desc: Documentary examining the killing of British tourist Peter Falconio in Australia's Northern Territory in July 2001, exploring the details and questions that surround this infamous case. In this first episode, Andrew Fraser, formerly one of Australia's foremost criminal defence lawyers, begins the reinvestigation into the Peter Falconio story, visits the Outback crime scene and speaks to a leading forensic scientist and witnesses from the time of the murder, who raise serious questions about evidence in the case

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to the Greek at the Harbour restaurant in southern California, where owner Makis Mikelatos works 17 hours a day, seven days a week, but is unwilling to let his son Ari take the reins. Gordon sets out to get to the bottom of Makis's reluctance to teach his son, and uncovers a shocking family secret

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three Birmingham couples compete to host the best dinner party and win a cash prize. Amanda and her husband Anthony kick off the competition with an ambitious menu, before it's the turn of festival lover Oli and his girlfriend LC, who prepare a vegetarian menu for their guests. However, their evening is tainted by Anthony, who upsets several of the guests with an untoward remark. Civil partners Steve and Sean try to put the week's tension behind them as they focus on winning the cash prize

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: Tom Allen and Liam Charles bring together the three best teams from both sets of heats, whose first challenge is to create 24 perfectly identical almond pithivier pies and individual savoury brioche, all in just three and a half hours. Then, judges Benoit Blin and Cherish Finden ask them to produce a striking sugar showpiece, inspired by a futuristic jungle theme, along with two types of confectionery of their choice

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: The programme celebrates the great British breakfast with a look back at some of the UK's favourite brekkie-based investigations, including when Jimmy Doherty traced the roots of baked beans back to the early settlers in America, and solved the puzzle of what kippers actually are. There is also a look at when Matt Tebbutt delved into the surprising science behind why nuts always rise to the top of his muesli, as he discovered how the same principle can save a skier's life in an avalanche

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: George follows the progress of a couple who want to transform two dilapidated air-raid shelters in their garden into his and hers sheds - but are arguing over who gets which one. He also meets the youngest designer ever featured on the show. Twelve-year-old Guinnie is taking her school project, to design the ultimate party shed, off the page and into her back garden - with the help of her builder dad. Will Hardie continues work on the floating beach hut and visits a million-pound beach house, to find out what is possible if money is no object, and in Germany, George explores an old water tower that has been transformed into a luxury home

programme.title: Coach Trip
programme.desc: Brendan sends the tourists parasailing in Lanzarote, before an afternoon activity forces the more masculine of the group to get in touch with their feminine side

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.desc: Mr Burns' long-lost son reappears - and promptly tries to trick his dastardly dad into admitting he loves him by pretending to be kidnapped by the Simpsons. Featuring the guest voice of Rodney Dangerfield

On day two, laughter-loving Keith and his laid-back wife Maz dish out good, honest food, but with foodie guests judging them, they're a bit worried. Dan and Les, who've been married for 17 years, host last. Self-confessed food snob Dan has devised a starter with eight separate elements. Will their risky choices and haute cuisine appeal to their guests?

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Mr Burns hires the Simpsons to play his live-in virtual reality family, but excludes Homer so that he can play the role of father. Homer, feeling all alone, finds a new best friend in girl-next-door Julia, who eats, drinks and thinks like him

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
